261: // transferPromptToCli moves QueuedPrompt into the tmux session's initialPrompt
262: // for programs that support CLI prompt injection. For unsupported programs,
263: // QueuedPrompt stays set so the send-keys fallback fires.
264: func (i *Instance) transferPromptToCli() {
265: 	if i.QueuedPrompt != "" && programSupportsCliPrompt(i.Program) {
266: 		i.tmuxSession.SetInitialPrompt(i.QueuedPrompt)
267: 		i.QueuedPrompt = ""
268: 	}
269: }

274: 		i.tmuxSession.SetTaskEnv(i.TaskNumber, i.WaveNumber, i.PeerCount)
275: 	}

278: // buildTitleOpts maps an Instance's metadata to TitleOpts for BuildTitle.
279: func buildTitleOpts(inst *Instance) opencodesession.TitleOpts {
280: 	planName := ""
281: 	if inst.PlanFile != "" {
282: 		planName = planstate.DisplayName(inst.PlanFile)
283: 	}
284: 	return opencodesession.TitleOpts{
285: 		PlanName:      planName,
286: 		AgentType:     inst.AgentType,
287: 		WaveNumber:    inst.WaveNumber,
288: 		TaskNumber:    inst.TaskNumber,
289: 		InstanceTitle: inst.Title,

293: // configureSessionTitle sets the desired opencode session title and the
294: // callback that writes it to the DB after the session becomes ready.
295: // It is a no-op for non-opencode programs.
296: func (i *Instance) configureSessionTitle() {
297: 	if i.tmuxSession == nil || !strings.HasSuffix(i.Program, "opencode") {
298: 		return
299: 	}
300: 	opts := buildTitleOpts(i)
301: 	title := opencodesession.BuildTitle(opts)
302: 	i.tmuxSession.SetSessionTitle(title)
303: 	i.tmuxSession.SetTitleFunc(func(workDir string, beforeStart time.Time, t string) {
304: 		if err := opencodesession.SetTitleDirect(workDir, beforeStart, t); err != nil {
305: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("opencodesession: set title: %v", err)
306: 		}
307: 	})
308: }

310: // Kill terminates the instance and cleans up all resources
311: func (i *Instance) Kill() error {
312: 	if !i.started {
313: 		// If instance was never started, just return success
314: 		return nil
315: 	}
316: 
317: 	var errs []error
318: 
319: 	// Always try to cleanup both resources, even if one fails
320: 	// Clean up tmux session first since it's using the git worktree
321: 	if i.tmuxSession != nil {
322: 		if err := i.tmuxSession.Close(); err != nil {
323: 			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to close tmux session: %w", err))
324: 		}
325: 	}
326: 
327: 	// Then clean up git worktree (skip if shared — topic owns the worktree)
328: 	if i.gitWorktree != nil && !i.sharedWorktree {
329: 		if err := i.gitWorktree.Cleanup(); err != nil {
330: 			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to cleanup git worktree: %w", err))
331: 		}
332: 	}
333: 
334: 	return errors.Join(errs...)
335: }
336: 
337: // StopTmux terminates the tmux session only. Does not touch the worktree,
338: // persistence, or plan state. The instance stays in the list as stopped.
339: func (i *Instance) StopTmux() {
340: 	if i.tmuxSession != nil {
341: 		_ = i.tmuxSession.Close()
342: 	}
343: }
344: 
345: // Pause stops the tmux session and removes the worktree, preserving the branch
346: func (i *Instance) Pause() error {
347: 	if !i.started {
348: 		return fmt.Errorf("cannot pause instance that has not been started")
349: 	}
350: 	if i.Status == Paused {
351: 		return fmt.Errorf("instance is already paused")
352: 	}
353: 
354: 	var errs []error
355: 
356: 	if !i.sharedWorktree && i.gitWorktree != nil {
357: 		// Check if there are any changes to commit
358: 		if dirty, err := i.gitWorktree.IsDirty(); err != nil {
359: 			errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to check if worktree is dirty: %w", err))
360: 			log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
361: 		} else if dirty {
362: 			// Commit changes locally (without pushing to GitHub)
363: 			commitMsg := fmt.Sprintf("[kas] update from '%s' on %s (paused)", i.Title, time.Now().Format(time.RFC822))
364: 			if err := i.gitWorktree.CommitChanges(commitMsg); err != nil {
365: 				errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to commit changes: %w", err))
366: 				log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
367: 				// Return early if we can't commit changes to avoid corrupted state
368: 				return errors.Join(errs...)
369: 			}
370: 		}
371: 	}
372: 
373: 	// Detach from tmux session instead of closing to preserve session output
374: 	if err := i.tmuxSession.DetachSafely(); err != nil {
375: 		errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to detach tmux session: %w", err))
376: 		log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
377: 		// Continue with pause process even if detach fails
378: 	}
379: 
380: 	if !i.sharedWorktree && i.gitWorktree != nil {
381: 		// Check if worktree exists before trying to remove it
382: 		if _, err := os.Stat(i.gitWorktree.GetWorktreePath()); err == nil {
383: 			// Remove worktree but keep branch
384: 			if err := i.gitWorktree.Remove(); err != nil {
385: 				errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to remove git worktree: %w", err))
386: 				log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
387: 				return errors.Join(errs...)
388: 			}
389: 
390: 			// Only prune if remove was successful
391: 			if err := i.gitWorktree.Prune(); err != nil {
392: 				errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("failed to prune git worktrees: %w", err))
393: 				log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
394: 				return errors.Join(errs...)
395: 			}
396: 		}
397: 	}
398: 
399: 	if err := errors.Join(errs...); err != nil {
400: 		log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
401: 		return err
402: 	}
403: 
404: 	i.SetStatus(Paused)
405: 	if i.gitWorktree != nil {
406: 		_ = clipboard.WriteAll(i.gitWorktree.GetBranchName())
407: 	}
408: 	return nil
409: }
410: 
411: // AdoptOrphanTmuxSession connects this instance to an existing orphaned tmux session
412: // identified by its raw tmux name. No worktree is created — the session keeps its
413: // existing working directory.
414: func (i *Instance) AdoptOrphanTmuxSession(tmuxName string) error {
415: 	ts := tmux.NewTmuxSessionFromExisting(tmuxName, i.Program, i.SkipPermissions)
416: 	i.tmuxSession = ts
417: 	if err := ts.Restore(); err != nil {
418: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to adopt orphan session %s: %w", tmuxName, err)
419: 	}
420: 	i.started = true
421: 	i.SetStatus(Ready)
422: 	return nil
423: }
424: 
425: // Resume recreates the worktree and restarts the tmux session
426: func (i *Instance) Resume() error {
427: 	if !i.started {
428: 		return fmt.Errorf("cannot resume instance that has not been started")
429: 	}
430: 	if i.Status != Paused {
431: 		return fmt.Errorf("can only resume paused instances")
432: 	}
433: 
434: 	// Check if branch is checked out
435: 	if checked, err := i.gitWorktree.IsBranchCheckedOut(); err != nil {
436: 		log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
437: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to check if branch is checked out: %w", err)
438: 	} else if checked {
439: 		return fmt.Errorf("cannot resume: branch is checked out, please switch to a different branch")
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	// Setup git worktree
443: 	if err := i.gitWorktree.Setup(); err != nil {
444: 		log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
445: 		return fmt.Errorf("failed to setup git worktree: %w", err)
446: 	}
447: 
448: 	// Check if tmux session still exists from pause, otherwise create new one
449: 	if i.tmuxSession.DoesSessionExist() {
450: 		// Session exists, just restore PTY connection to it
451: 		if err := i.tmuxSession.Restore(); err != nil {
452: 			log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
453: 			// If restore fails, fall back to creating new session
454: 			if err := i.tmuxSession.Start(i.gitWorktree.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
455: 				log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
456: 				// Cleanup git worktree if tmux session creation fails
457: 				if cleanupErr := i.gitWorktree.Cleanup(); cleanupErr != nil {
458: 					err = fmt.Errorf("%v (cleanup error: %v)", err, cleanupErr)
459: 					log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
460: 				}
461: 				return fmt.Errorf("failed to start new session: %w", err)
462: 			}
463: 		}
464: 	} else {
465: 		// Create new tmux session
466: 		if err := i.tmuxSession.Start(i.gitWorktree.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
467: 			log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
468: 			// Cleanup git worktree if tmux session creation fails
469: 			if cleanupErr := i.gitWorktree.Cleanup(); cleanupErr != nil {
470: 				err = fmt.Errorf("%v (cleanup error: %v)", err, cleanupErr)
471: 				log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
472: 			}
473: 			return fmt.Errorf("failed to start new session: %w", err)
474: 		}
475: 	}
476: 
477: 	i.SetStatus(Running)
478: 	return nil
479: }

(End of file - total 479 lines)

340: func newHome(ctx context.Context, program string, autoYes bool) *home {
341: 	// Load application config
342: 	appConfig := config.LoadConfig()
343: 
344: 	// Load application state
345: 	appState := config.LoadState()
346: 
347: 	// Initialize storage
348: 	storage, err := session.NewStorage(appState)
349: 	if err != nil {
350: 		fmt.Printf("Failed to initialize storage: %v\n", err)
351: 		os.Exit(1)
352: 	}
353: 
354: 	activeRepoPath, err := filepath.Abs(".")
355: 	if err != nil {
356: 		fmt.Printf("Failed to get current directory: %v\n", err)
357: 		os.Exit(1)
358: 	}
359: 
360: 	project := filepath.Base(activeRepoPath)
361: 	h := &home{
362: 		ctx:                   ctx,
363: 		spinner:               spinner.New(spinner.WithSpinner(spinner.Dot)),
364: 		menu:                  ui.NewMenu(),
365: 		auditPane:             ui.NewAuditPane(),
366: 		statusBar:             ui.NewStatusBar(),
367: 		tabbedWindow:          ui.NewTabbedWindow(ui.NewPreviewPane(), ui.NewDiffPane(), ui.NewInfoPane()),
368: 		storage:               storage,
369: 		appConfig:             appConfig,
370: 		program:               program,
371: 		autoYes:               autoYes,
372: 		state:                 stateDefault,
373: 		appState:              appState,
374: 		activeRepoPath:        activeRepoPath,
375: 		planStateDir:          filepath.Join(activeRepoPath, "docs", "plans"),
376: 		signalsDir:            filepath.Join(activeRepoPath, ".kasmos", "signals"),
377: 		planStoreProject:      project,
378: 		instanceFinalizers:    make(map[*session.Instance]func()),
379: 		waveOrchestrators:     make(map[string]*WaveOrchestrator),
380: 		plannerPrompted:       make(map[string]bool),
381: 		pendingReviewFeedback: make(map[string]string),
382: 	}
383: 
384: 	// Always start an embedded plan store server. This gives us a local SQLite
385: 	// DB as the single source of truth without requiring a separate process.
386: 	dbPath := planstore.ResolvedDBPath()
387: 	embSrv, err := planstore.StartEmbedded(dbPath, 0)
388: 	if err != nil {
389: 		fmt.Printf("Failed to start embedded plan store: %v\n", err)
390: 		os.Exit(1)
391: 	}
392: 	h.embeddedServer = embSrv
393: 
394: 	// Default: use the embedded server's URL for the plan store client.
395: 	planStoreURL := embSrv.URL()
396: 	remoteStoreUnreachable := false
397: 
398: 	// If a remote plan store is configured, use that URL instead (multi-machine
399: 	// access over tailscale, etc.). The embedded server still runs for audit log
400: 	// DB access via its SQLite store.
401: 	if appConfig.PlanStore != "" {
402: 		remoteStore := planstore.NewHTTPStore(appConfig.PlanStore, project)
403: 		if pingErr := remoteStore.Ping(); pingErr != nil {
404: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("remote plan store unreachable: %v — falling back to embedded", pingErr)
405: 			remoteStoreUnreachable = true
406: 			// planStoreURL stays as the embedded server URL
407: 		} else {
408: 			planStoreURL = appConfig.PlanStore
409: 		}
410: 	}
411: 
412: 	h.planStore = planstore.NewHTTPStore(planStoreURL, project)
413: 	h.fsm = planfsm.New(h.planStore, project, h.planStateDir)
414: 
415: 	// One-time migration: import plan-state.json into the DB if it exists.
416: 	// Use the embedded store directly (bypasses HTTP round-trip).
417: 	// Only runs when plan-state.json is present; subsequent boots skip this.
418: 	planStateJSON := filepath.Join(h.planStateDir, "plan-state.json")
419: 	if _, statErr := os.Stat(planStateJSON); statErr == nil {
420: 		migrated, migrateErr := planstore.MigrateFromJSON(embSrv.Store(), project, h.planStateDir)
421: 		if migrateErr != nil {
422: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("plan-state.json migration failed: %v", migrateErr)
423: 		} else {
424: 			log.InfoLog.Printf("migrated %d plans from plan-state.json to DB", migrated)
425: 			if renameErr := os.Rename(planStateJSON, planStateJSON+".migrated"); renameErr != nil {
426: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("failed to rename plan-state.json after migration: %v", renameErr)
427: 			}
428: 		}
429: 	}
430: 
431: 	// Initialize audit logger. Always uses local SQLite regardless of plan
432: 	// store backend — audit events are purely local state.
433: 	if al, err := auditlog.NewSQLiteLogger(dbPath); err != nil {
434: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("audit logger init failed: %v", err)
435: 		h.auditLogger = auditlog.NopLogger()
436: 	} else {
437: 		h.auditLogger = al
438: 	}
439: 
440: 	h.nav = ui.NewNavigationPanel(&h.spinner)
441: 	h.toastManager = overlay.NewToastManager(&h.spinner)
442: 
443: 	// Show a warning toast if a remote plan store was configured but unreachable
444: 	// (we fell back to the embedded server).
445: 	if remoteStoreUnreachable {
446: 		h.toastManager.Error("remote plan store unreachable — using embedded store")
447: 	}
448: 
449: 	permCacheDir := filepath.Join(activeRepoPath, ".kasmos")
450: 	permStore, err := config.NewSQLitePermissionStore(dbPath)
451: 	if err != nil {
452: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("permission store init failed: %v", err)
453: 	} else {
454: 		if migrateErr := config.MigratePermissionCache(permCacheDir, project, permStore); migrateErr != nil {
455: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("permission cache migration failed: %v", migrateErr)
456: 		}
457: 		h.permissionStore = permStore
458: 	}
459: 	h.permissionHandled = make(map[*session.Instance]string)
460: 
461: 	h.tabbedWindow.SetAnimateBanner(appConfig.AnimateBanner)
462: 	h.setFocusSlot(slotNav)
463: 	h.loadPlanState()
464: 
465: 	// Load saved instances
466: 	instances, err := storage.LoadInstances()
467: 	if err != nil {
468: 		fmt.Printf("Failed to load instances: %v\n", err)
469: 		os.Exit(1)
470: 	}
471: 
472: 	h.allInstances = instances
473: 
474: 	// Add instances matching active repo to the nav
475: 	for _, instance := range instances {
476: 		repoPath := instance.GetRepoPath()
477: 		if repoPath == "" || repoPath == h.activeRepoPath {
478: 			h.nav.AddInstance(instance)()
479: 			if autoYes {
480: 				instance.AutoYes = true
481: 			}
482: 		}
483: 	}
484: 
485: 	h.updateSidebarPlans()
486: 
487: 	// Reconstruct in-memory wave orchestrators for plans that were mid-wave
488: 	// when kasmos was last restarted. Must run after loadPlanState and instance load.
489: 	h.rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators()
490: 
491: 	return h
492: }
493: 
494: // activeProject returns the project name derived from the active repo path.
495: // This matches how planstore derives the project name (filepath.Base of the repo path).
496: func (m *home) activeProject() string {
497: 	return filepath.Base(m.activeRepoPath)
498: }
499: 
500: // isUserInOverlay returns true when the user is actively interacting with
501: // any modal overlay. Used to prevent async metadata-tick handlers from
502: // clobbering the active overlay by showing a confirmation dialog.
503: func (m *home) isUserInOverlay() bool {
504: 	switch m.state {
505: 	case stateDefault:
506: 		return false
507: 	}
508: 	return true
509: }
510: 
511: // updateHandleWindowSizeEvent sets the sizes of the components.
512: // The components will try to render inside their bounds.
513: func (m *home) updateHandleWindowSizeEvent(msg tea.WindowSizeMsg) {
514: 	// Two-column layout: nav + preview
515: 	var navWidth int
516: 	if m.sidebarHidden {
517: 		navWidth = 0
518: 	} else {
519: 		navWidth = msg.Width * 30 / 100
520: 		if navWidth < 25 {
521: 			navWidth = 25
522: 		}
523: 	}
524: 	tabsWidth := msg.Width - navWidth
525: 
526: 	// Keep the keybind rail compact and give the saved rows to the three columns.
527: 	menuHeight := 1
528: 	if msg.Height < 2 {
529: 		menuHeight = 0
530: 	}
531: 	statusBarHeight := 1
532: 	contentHeight := msg.Height - menuHeight - statusBarHeight
533: 	if contentHeight < 1 {
534: 		contentHeight = 1
535: 	}
536: 	// Detect actual terminal resize vs spurious tea.WindowSize() side-effects.
537: 	termResized := msg.Width != m.termWidth || msg.Height != m.termHeight
538: 
539: 	m.termWidth = msg.Width
540: 	m.termHeight = msg.Height
541: 	m.toastManager.SetSize(msg.Width, msg.Height)
542: 	if m.statusBar != nil {
543: 		m.statusBar.SetSize(msg.Width)
544: 	}
545: 
546: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetSize(tabsWidth, contentHeight)
547: 
548: 	// Nav panel gets full content height — audit pane is rendered inside its border.
549: 	m.nav.SetSize(navWidth, contentHeight)
550: 	if m.auditPane != nil && m.auditPane.Visible() && navWidth > 0 {
551: 		// Size audit pane for the nav panel's inner content area.
552: 		// border (2) + border padding (2) + item padding (2) = 6
553: 		auditInnerW := navWidth - 6
554: 		// Pass full content height — the nav panel clamps to whatever space
555: 		// remains below the list content (active/plans sections + legend).
556: 		auditH := contentHeight
557: 		if !m.auditBootstrapped {
558: 			m.refreshAuditPane() // load historical events on first render
559: 			m.auditBootstrapped = true
560: 		}
561: 		m.auditPane.SetSize(auditInnerW, auditH)
562: 		m.nav.SetAuditView(m.auditPane.String(), auditH)
563: 	} else {
564: 		m.nav.SetAuditView("", 0)
565: 		if m.auditPane != nil {
566: 			m.auditPane.SetSize(0, 0)
567: 		}
568: 	}
569: 
570: 	// Store for mouse hit-testing
571: 	m.navWidth = navWidth
572: 	m.tabsWidth = tabsWidth
573: 	m.contentHeight = contentHeight
574: 
575: 	if navWidth == 0 && m.focusSlot == slotNav {
576: 		m.setFocusSlot(slotAgent)
577: 	}
578: 
579: 	// Only resize overlays when the terminal dimensions actually changed.
580: 	// Many handlers emit tea.WindowSize() as a batched side-effect (e.g.
581: 	// instanceStartedMsg) — those fire with the same dimensions and should
582: 	// not overwrite the overlay's explicit sizing.
583: 	if m.textInputOverlay != nil && termResized {
584: 		m.textInputOverlay.SetSize(int(float32(msg.Width)*0.6), int(float32(msg.Height)*0.4))
585: 	}
586: 	if m.textOverlay != nil && termResized {
587: 		m.textOverlay.SetWidth(int(float32(msg.Width) * 0.6))
588: 	}
589: 
590: 	previewWidth, previewHeight := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
591: 	if m.previewTerminal != nil {
592: 		m.previewTerminal.Resize(previewWidth, previewHeight)
593: 	}
594: 	if err := m.nav.SetSessionPreviewSize(previewWidth, previewHeight); err != nil {
595: 		log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
596: 	}
597: 	m.menu.SetSize(msg.Width, menuHeight)
598: }
599: 
600: func (m *home) Init() tea.Cmd {
601: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventSessionStarted, "kasmos started")
602: 
603: 	// Upon starting, we want to start the spinner. Whenever we get a spinner.TickMsg, we
604: 	// update the spinner, which sends a new spinner.TickMsg. I think this lasts forever lol.
605: 	return tea.Batch(
606: 		m.spinner.Tick,
607: 		func() tea.Msg {
608: 			time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
609: 			return previewTickMsg{}
610: 		},
611: 		tickUpdateMetadataCmd,
612: 		m.toastTickCmd(),
613: 		detectClickUpCmd(m.activeRepoPath),
614: 	)
615: }
616: 
617: func (m *home) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
618: 	switch msg := msg.(type) {
619: 	case overlay.ToastTickMsg:
620: 		m.toastManager.Tick()
621: 		if m.toastManager.HasActiveToasts() {
622: 			return m, m.toastTickCmd()
623: 		}
624: 		return m, nil
625: 	case prCreatedMsg:
626: 		m.toastManager.Resolve(m.pendingPRToastID, overlay.ToastSuccess, "PR created!")
627: 		m.pendingPRToastID = ""
628: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventPRCreated, fmt.Sprintf("PR created: %s", msg.prTitle),
629: 			auditlog.WithInstance(msg.instanceTitle),
630: 		)
631: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
632: 	case prErrorMsg:
633: 		log.ErrorLog.Printf("%v", msg.err)
634: 		m.toastManager.Resolve(msg.id, overlay.ToastError, msg.err.Error())
635: 		m.pendingPRToastID = ""
636: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
637: 	case planRenderedMsg:
638: 		if msg.err != nil {
639: 			return m, m.handleError(msg.err)
640: 		}
641: 		m.cachedPlanFile = msg.planFile
642: 		m.cachedPlanRendered = msg.rendered
643: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
644: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetDocumentContent(msg.rendered)
645: 		return m, nil
646: 	case previewTickMsg:
647: 		// If previewTerminal is active, render from it (zero-latency VT emulator).
648: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil && !m.tabbedWindow.IsDocumentMode() {
649: 			if content, changed := m.previewTerminal.Render(); changed {
650: 				m.tabbedWindow.SetPreviewContent(content)
651: 			}
652: 		} else if m.previewTerminal == nil && !m.tabbedWindow.IsDocumentMode() {
653: 			// No terminal — show appropriate fallback state.
654: 			selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
655: 			if selected != nil && selected.Started() && selected.Status != session.Paused && selected.Status != session.Loading {
656: 				// Instance is running but terminal hasn't attached yet — show connecting indicator.
657: 				m.tabbedWindow.SetConnectingState()
658: 			} else {
659: 				// nil, Loading, or Paused — delegate to UpdatePreview which renders the
660: 				// correct fallback (banner, progress bar, or paused message).
661: 				if err := m.tabbedWindow.UpdatePreview(selected); err != nil {
662: 					log.ErrorLog.Printf("preview update error: %v", err)
663: 				}
664: 			}
665: 		}
666: 		// Advance spring animation every tick (20fps)
667: 		m.tabbedWindow.TickSpring()
668: 		// Banner animation (only when no terminal is active / fallback showing).
669: 		m.previewTickCount++
670: 		if m.previewTickCount%20 == 0 {
671: 			m.tabbedWindow.TickBanner()
672: 		}
673: 		// Use event-driven wakeup when terminal is live, fall back to 50ms poll otherwise.
674: 		term := m.previewTerminal
675: 		return m, func() tea.Msg {
676: 			if term != nil {
677: 				term.WaitForRender(16 * time.Millisecond)
678: 			} else {
679: 				time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
680: 			}
681: 			return previewTickMsg{}
682: 		}
683: 	case keyupMsg:
684: 		m.menu.ClearKeydown()
685: 		return m, nil
686: 	case clickUpDetectedMsg:
687: 		m.clickUpConfig = &msg.Config
688: 		m.nav.SetClickUpAvailable(true)
689: 		return m, nil
690: 	case clickUpSearchResultMsg:
691: 		if msg.Err != nil {
692: 			// Check if the error is a multiple-workspaces error — show picker instead of failing.
693: 			var mwErr *clickup.MultipleWorkspacesError
694: 			if errors.As(msg.Err, &mwErr) && len(mwErr.WorkspaceIDs) > 0 {
695: 				m.clickUpPendingQuery = msg.Query
696: 				m.state = stateClickUpWorkspacePicker
697: 				// Build picker labels: "name (id)" when names are available, bare id otherwise.
698: 				items := make([]string, len(mwErr.WorkspaceIDs))
699: 				m.clickUpWorkspaceMap = make(map[string]string, len(mwErr.WorkspaceIDs))
700: 				for i, id := range mwErr.WorkspaceIDs {
701: 					if name, ok := mwErr.WorkspaceNames[id]; ok && name != "" {
702: 						label := name + " (" + id + ")"
703: 						items[i] = label
704: 						m.clickUpWorkspaceMap[label] = id
705: 					} else {
706: 						items[i] = id
707: 						m.clickUpWorkspaceMap[id] = id
708: 					}
709: 				}
710: 				m.pickerOverlay = overlay.NewPickerOverlay("select clickup workspace", items)
711: 				return m, nil
712: 			}
713: 			m.toastManager.Error("clickup search failed: " + msg.Err.Error())
714: 			m.state = stateDefault
715: 			return m, m.toastTickCmd()
716: 		}
717: 		if len(msg.Results) == 0 {
718: 			m.toastManager.Info("no clickup tasks found")
719: 			m.state = stateDefault
720: 			return m, m.toastTickCmd()
721: 		}
722: 		m.clickUpResults = msg.Results
723: 		items := make([]string, len(msg.Results))
724: 		for i, r := range msg.Results {
725: 			label := r.ID + " · " + r.Name
726: 			if r.Status != "" {
727: 				label += " (" + r.Status + ")"
728: 			}
729: 			if r.ListName != "" {
730: 				label += " — " + r.ListName
731: 			}
732: 			items[i] = label
733: 		}
734: 		m.state = stateClickUpPicker
735: 		m.pickerOverlay = overlay.NewPickerOverlay("select clickup task", items)
736: 		return m, nil
737: 	case tickUpdateMetadataMessage:
738: 		// Snapshot the instance list for the goroutine. The slice header is
739: 		// copied but the pointers are shared — CollectMetadata only reads
740: 		// instance fields that don't change between ticks (started, Status,
741: 		// tmuxSession, gitWorktree, Program).
742: 		instances := m.nav.GetInstances()
743: 		snapshots := make([]*session.Instance, len(instances))
744: 		copy(snapshots, instances)
745: 		planStateDir := m.planStateDir // snapshot for goroutine
746: 		signalsDir := m.signalsDir     // snapshot for goroutine
747: 		store := m.planStore           // snapshot for goroutine
748: 		project := m.planStoreProject  // snapshot for goroutine
749: 
750: 		return m, func() tea.Msg {
751: 			results := make([]instanceMetadata, 0, len(snapshots))
752: 			for _, inst := range snapshots {
753: 				if !inst.Started() || inst.Paused() {
754: 					continue
755: 				}
756: 				md := inst.CollectMetadata()
757: 				results = append(results, instanceMetadata{
758: 					Title:              inst.Title,
759: 					Content:            md.Content,
760: 					ContentCaptured:    md.ContentCaptured,
761: 					Updated:            md.Updated,
762: 					HasPrompt:          md.HasPrompt,
763: 					DiffStats:          md.DiffStats,
764: 					CPUPercent:         md.CPUPercent,
765: 					MemMB:              md.MemMB,
766: 					ResourceUsageValid: md.ResourceUsageValid,
767: 					TmuxAlive:          md.TmuxAlive,
768: 					PermissionPrompt:   md.PermissionPrompt,
769: 				})
770: 			}
771: 
772: 			// Load plan state — moved here from the synchronous Update handler
773: 			// to avoid blocking the event loop every 500ms.
774: 			// Always reads from the store (embedded or remote) — no JSON fallback.
775: 			var ps *planstate.PlanState
776: 			if planStateDir != "" {
777: 				var loaded *planstate.PlanState
778: 				var err error
779: 				loaded, err = planstate.Load(store, project, planStateDir)
780: 				if err != nil {
781: 					log.WarningLog.Printf("could not load plan state: %v", err)
782: 				} else {
783: 					ps = loaded
784: 				}
785: 			}
786: 
787: 			// Scan signals from the project-local signals directory (.kasmos/signals/).
788: 			var signals []planfsm.Signal
789: 			if signalsDir != "" {
790: 				signals = planfsm.ScanSignals(signalsDir)
791: 			}
792: 
793: 			// Also scan signals from active worktrees — agents write
794: 			// sentinel files relative to their CWD which is the worktree,
795: 			// not the main repo. Worktrees use .kasmos/signals/ as well.
796: 			seen := make(map[string]bool)
797: 			for _, sig := range signals {
798: 				seen[sig.Key()] = true
799: 			}
800: 			for _, inst := range snapshots {
801: 				wt := inst.GetWorktreePath()
802: 				if wt == "" {
803: 					continue
804: 				}
805: 				wtSignalsDir := filepath.Join(wt, ".kasmos", "signals")
806: 				for _, sig := range planfsm.ScanSignals(wtSignalsDir) {
807: 					if !seen[sig.Key()] {
808: 						seen[sig.Key()] = true
809: 						signals = append(signals, sig)
810: 					}
811: 				}
812: 			}
813: 
814: 			var waveSignals []planfsm.WaveSignal
815: 			if signalsDir != "" {
816: 				waveSignals = planfsm.ScanWaveSignals(signalsDir)
817: 			}
818: 
819: 			tmuxCount := tmux.CountKasSessions(cmd2.MakeExecutor())
820: 			time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)
821: 			return metadataResultMsg{Results: results, PlanState: ps, Signals: signals, WaveSignals: waveSignals, TmuxSessionCount: tmuxCount}
822: 		}
823: 	case metadataResultMsg:
824: 		// Process agent sentinel signals — feed to FSM and consume sentinel files.
825: 		// Done in Update (main goroutine) so FSM writes are never concurrent.
826: 		// Side-effect cmds (reviewer/coder spawns) are collected and batched below.
827: 		var signalCmds []tea.Cmd
828: 		for _, sig := range msg.Signals {
829: 			// Guard: if a wave orchestrator is active for this plan, ignore
830: 			// implement-finished signals. Wave task agents may write this sentinel
831: 			// after completing their individual task, but the wave orchestrator
832: 			// owns the implementing→reviewing transition. Without this guard,
833: 			// the first task to finish prematurely triggers review and pauses
834: 			// sibling tasks, spawning a "applying fixes" coder alongside the
835: 			// reviewer.
836: 			if sig.Event == planfsm.ImplementFinished {
837: 				if _, hasOrch := m.waveOrchestrators[sig.PlanFile]; hasOrch {
838: 					log.WarningLog.Printf("ignoring implement-finished signal for %q — wave orchestrator active", sig.PlanFile)
839: 					planfsm.ConsumeSignal(sig)
840: 					continue
841: 				}
842: 			}
843: 
844: 			if err := m.fsm.Transition(sig.PlanFile, sig.Event); err != nil {
845: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("signal %s for %s rejected: %v", sig.Event, sig.PlanFile, err)
846: 				planfsm.ConsumeSignal(sig)
847: 				continue
848: 			}
849: 			planfsm.ConsumeSignal(sig)
850: 
851: 			// Side effects: spawn agents in response to successful transitions.
852: 			switch sig.Event {
853: 			case planfsm.ImplementFinished:
854: 				// Pause the coder that wrote this signal.
855: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
856: 					if inst.PlanFile == sig.PlanFile && inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypeCoder {
857: 						inst.ImplementationComplete = true
858: 						_ = inst.Pause()
859: 						break
860: 					}
861: 				}
862: 				if cmd := m.spawnReviewer(sig.PlanFile); cmd != nil {
863: 					signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
864: 				}
865: 			case planfsm.ReviewApproved:
866: 				planName := planstate.DisplayName(sig.PlanFile)
867: 				m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, "reviewing → done (review approved)",
868: 					auditlog.WithPlan(sig.PlanFile))
869: 				m.toastManager.Success(fmt.Sprintf("review approved: %s", planName))
870: 				// Kill the reviewer instance — it's done.
871: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
872: 					if inst.PlanFile == sig.PlanFile && inst.IsReviewer {
873: 						_ = inst.Kill()
874: 						break
875: 					}
876: 				}
877: 			case planfsm.ReviewChangesRequested:
878: 				feedback := sig.Body
879: 				m.pendingReviewFeedback[sig.PlanFile] = feedback
880: 				// Pause the reviewer that wrote this signal.
881: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
882: 					if inst.PlanFile == sig.PlanFile && inst.IsReviewer {
883: 						_ = inst.Pause()
884: 						break
885: 					}
886: 				}
887: 				if cmd := m.spawnCoderWithFeedback(sig.PlanFile, feedback); cmd != nil {
888: 					signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
889: 				}
890: 			case planfsm.PlannerFinished:
891: 				capturedPlanFile := sig.PlanFile
892: 				// Ingest the plan content from the agent's worktree into the DB.
893: 				// Planners run on the main branch, so the plan file is in activeRepoPath.
894: 				m.ingestPlanContent(capturedPlanFile, m.activeRepoPath)
895: 				if m.plannerPrompted[capturedPlanFile] {
896: 					break
897: 				}
898: 				if m.isUserInOverlay() {
899: 					// Overlay is active — defer the dialog to the next tick
900: 					// instead of silently dropping it. The sentinel has already
901: 					// been consumed and the FSM transitioned; we must not lose
902: 					// the "show dialog" side effect.
903: 					m.deferredPlannerDialogs = append(m.deferredPlannerDialogs, capturedPlanFile)
904: 					break
905: 				}
906: 				// Focus the planner instance so the user sees its output behind the overlay.
907: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
908: 					if inst.PlanFile == sig.PlanFile && inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypePlanner {
909: 						if cmd := m.focusInstanceForOverlay(inst); cmd != nil {
910: 							signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
911: 						}
912: 						m.pendingPlannerInstanceTitle = inst.Title
913: 						break
914: 					}
915: 				}
916: 				m.pendingPlannerPlanFile = capturedPlanFile
917: 				m.confirmAction(
918: 					fmt.Sprintf("plan '%s' is ready. start implementation?", planstate.DisplayName(capturedPlanFile)),
919: 					func() tea.Msg {
920: 						return plannerCompleteMsg{planFile: capturedPlanFile}
921: 					},
922: 				)
923: 			}
924: 		}
925: 		if len(msg.Signals) > 0 {
926: 			m.loadPlanState() // refresh after signal processing
927: 		}
928: 
929: 		// Retry deferred PlannerFinished dialogs — show the first queued plan
930: 		// whose dialog was skipped because an overlay was active at signal time.
931: 		if len(m.deferredPlannerDialogs) > 0 && !m.isUserInOverlay() {
932: 			planFile := m.deferredPlannerDialogs[0]
933: 			m.deferredPlannerDialogs = m.deferredPlannerDialogs[1:]
934: 			if !m.plannerPrompted[planFile] {
935: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
936: 					if inst.PlanFile == planFile && inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypePlanner {
937: 						if cmd := m.focusInstanceForOverlay(inst); cmd != nil {
938: 							signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
939: 						}
940: 						m.pendingPlannerInstanceTitle = inst.Title
941: 						break
942: 					}
943: 				}
944: 				m.pendingPlannerPlanFile = planFile
945: 				m.confirmAction(
946: 					fmt.Sprintf("plan '%s' is ready. start implementation?", planstate.DisplayName(planFile)),
947: 					func() tea.Msg {
948: 						return plannerCompleteMsg{planFile: planFile}
949: 					},
950: 				)
951: 			}
952: 		}
953: 
954: 		// Process wave signals — trigger implementation for specific waves.
955: 		for _, ws := range msg.WaveSignals {
956: 			planfsm.ConsumeWaveSignal(ws)
957: 
958: 			// Check if orchestrator already exists
959: 			if _, exists := m.waveOrchestrators[ws.PlanFile]; exists {
960: 				m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("wave already running for '%s'", planstate.DisplayName(ws.PlanFile)))
961: 				continue
962: 			}
963: 
964: 			// Read and parse the plan from store
965: 			content, err := m.planStore.GetContent(m.planStoreProject, ws.PlanFile)
966: 			if err != nil {
967: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("wave signal: could not read plan %s: %v", ws.PlanFile, err)
968: 				continue
969: 			}
970: 			plan, err := planparser.Parse(content)
971: 			if err != nil {
972: 				m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("plan '%s' has no wave headers", planstate.DisplayName(ws.PlanFile)))
973: 				continue
974: 			}
975: 
976: 			if ws.WaveNumber > len(plan.Waves) {
977: 				m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("plan has %d waves, requested wave %d", len(plan.Waves), ws.WaveNumber))
978: 				continue
979: 			}
980: 
981: 			entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(ws.PlanFile)
982: 			if !ok {
983: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("wave signal: plan %s not found in plan state", ws.PlanFile)
984: 				continue
985: 			}
986: 
987: 			orch := NewWaveOrchestrator(ws.PlanFile, plan)
988: 			m.waveOrchestrators[ws.PlanFile] = orch
989: 
990: 			// Fast-forward to the requested wave
991: 			for i := 1; i < ws.WaveNumber; i++ {
992: 				tasks := orch.StartNextWave()
993: 				for _, t := range tasks {
994: 					orch.MarkTaskComplete(t.Number)
995: 				}
996: 			}
997: 
998: 			mdl, cmd := m.startNextWave(orch, entry)
999: 			m = mdl.(*home)
1000: 			if cmd != nil {

1237: 						}

1301: 						m.audit(auditlog.EventWaveFailed,

106: 		data.Branch = selected.Branch
107: 		if selected.PlanFile != "" && m.planState != nil {
108: 			entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(selected.PlanFile)
109: 			if ok {
110: 				data.PlanName = planstate.DisplayName(selected.PlanFile)
111: 				data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
112: 			}
113: 		}
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	if data.Branch == "" {
117: 		data.Branch = "main"
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	return data
121: }
122: 
123: // computePlanStatuses builds per-plan instance status flags (running/notification)
124: // from the current instance list.
125: func (m *home) computePlanStatuses() map[string]ui.TopicStatus {
126: 	planStatuses := make(map[string]ui.TopicStatus)
127: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
128: 		if inst.PlanFile == "" {
129: 			continue
130: 		}
131: 		planSt := planStatuses[inst.PlanFile]
132: 		planStatuses[inst.PlanFile] = mergePlanStatus(planSt, inst, inst.Started())
133: 	}
134: 	return planStatuses
135: }
136: 
137: // updateNavPanelStatus recomputes plan instance statuses and triggers a row
138: // rebuild. Use this after instance mutations (kill, remove, pause) where the
139: // plan list itself hasn't changed. When updateSidebarPlans is also called,
140: // skip this — updateSidebarPlans already includes plan statuses in its rebuild.
141: func (m *home) updateNavPanelStatus() {
142: 	m.nav.SetItems(nil, nil, 0, nil, nil, m.computePlanStatuses())
143: }

145: // focusSlot constants for readability.
146: // Order matches tab layout: info (first), agent (second), diff (third).
147: const (
148: 	slotNav   = 0
149: 	slotInfo  = 1
150: 	slotAgent = 2
151: 	slotDiff  = 3
152: 	slotCount = 4
153: )
154: 
155: // setFocusSlot updates which pane has focus and syncs visual state.
156: func (m *home) setFocusSlot(slot int) {
157: 	m.focusSlot = slot
158: 	m.nav.SetFocused(slot == slotNav)
159: 	m.menu.SetFocusSlot(slot)
160: 
161: 	// Center pane is focused when any of the 3 center tabs is active.
162: 	centerFocused := slot >= slotInfo && slot <= slotDiff
163: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocused(centerFocused)
164: 
165: 	// When focusing a center tab, switch the visible tab to match and track which tab is focused.
166: 	if centerFocused {
167: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(slot - slotInfo) // slotInfo=1 → InfoTab=0, etc.
168: 	}
169: 	// focusedTab always tracks activeTab so the gradient header is visible
170: 	// regardless of which pane has keyboard focus.
171: }
172: 
173: // nextFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab forward (info → agent → diff → info).
174: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav); only the
175: // displayed tab changes. This is called by Tab and →.
176: func (m *home) nextFocusSlot() {
177: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
178: 	case ui.InfoTab:
179: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
180: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
181: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
182: 	default: // ui.DiffTab — wraps to info
183: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
184: 	}
185: }
186: 
187: // prevFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab backward (info → diff → agent → info).
188: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav).
189: func (m *home) prevFocusSlot() {
190: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
191: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
192: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
193: 	case ui.DiffTab:
194: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
195: 	default: // ui.InfoTab — wraps to diff
196: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
197: 	}
198: }
199: 
200: // enterFocusMode enters focus/insert mode and starts the fast preview ticker.
201: // enterFocusMode reuses the existing previewTerminal if it is already attached to
202: // the selected instance — entering focus just toggles key forwarding to the same
203: // terminal. Only spawns a new terminal if none is attached yet (rare fallback).
204: func (m *home) enterFocusMode() tea.Cmd {
205: 	m.tabbedWindow.ClearDocumentMode()
206: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
207: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused {
208: 		return nil
209: 	}
210: 
211: 	// If previewTerminal is already attached to this instance, just enter focus mode.
212: 	if m.previewTerminal != nil && m.previewTerminalInstance == selected.Title {
213: 		m.state = stateFocusAgent
214: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
215: 		m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
216: 		return nil
217: 	}
218: 
219: 	// No terminal yet (shouldn't normally happen) — spawn one synchronously-ish.
220: 	cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
221: 	if cols < 10 {
222: 		cols = 80
223: 	}
224: 	if rows < 5 {
225: 		rows = 24
226: 	}
227: 	term, err := selected.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
228: 	if err != nil {
229: 		return m.handleError(err)
230: 	}
231: 	m.previewTerminal = term
232: 	m.previewTerminalInstance = selected.Title
233: 	m.state = stateFocusAgent
234: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
235: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
236: 
237: 	return nil
238: }
239: 
240: // exitFocusMode resets focus state. previewTerminal stays alive — it continues
241: // rendering in normal preview mode after the user exits focus/insert mode.
242: func (m *home) exitFocusMode() {
243: 	// previewTerminal stays alive — it continues rendering in normal preview mode.
244: 	m.state = stateDefault
245: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(false)
246: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(false)
247: }
248: 
249: // switchToTab changes the visible center tab without stealing focus from the sidebar.
250: // The sidebar (slotNav) always retains keyboard focus.
251: func (m *home) switchToTab(name keys.KeyName) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
252: 	var targetTab int
253: 	switch name {
254: 	case keys.KeyTabAgent:
255: 		targetTab = ui.PreviewTab
256: 	case keys.KeyTabDiff:
257: 		targetTab = ui.DiffTab
258: 	case keys.KeyTabInfo:
259: 		targetTab = ui.InfoTab
260: 	default:
261: 		return m, nil
262: 	}
263: 
264: 	if m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() == targetTab {
265: 		return m, nil
266: 	}
267: 
268: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(targetTab)
269: 	return m, nil
270: }
271: 
272: // saveAllInstances saves allInstances (all repos) to storage.
273: // No-ops gracefully when storage is nil (e.g. in unit tests).
274: func (m *home) saveAllInstances() error {
275: 	if m.storage == nil {
276: 		return nil
277: 	}
278: 	return m.storage.SaveInstances(m.allInstances)
279: }
280: 
281: // removeFromAllInstances removes an instance from the master list by title.
282: func (m *home) removeFromAllInstances(title string) {
283: 	for i, inst := range m.allInstances {
284: 		if inst.Title == title {
285: 			m.allInstances = append(m.allInstances[:i], m.allInstances[i+1:]...)
286: 			return
287: 		}
288: 	}
289: }
290: 
291: // instanceChanged updates the preview pane, menu, and diff pane based on the selected instance.
292: // It returns a tea.Cmd when an async operation is needed (terminal spawn).
293: func (m *home) instanceChanged() tea.Cmd {
294: 	// selected may be nil
295: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
296: 
297: 	// Clear notification on the previously-viewed instance when the user
298: 	// navigates away. This prevents the item from jumping out of "attention"
299: 	// while the user is still looking at it.
300: 	if m.seenNotified != nil && m.seenNotified != selected {
301: 		m.seenNotified.Notified = false
302: 		m.seenNotified = nil
303: 		m.updateNavPanelStatus()
304: 	}
305: 	if selected != nil && selected.Notified {
306: 		m.seenNotified = selected
307: 	}
308: 
309: 	// Manage preview terminal lifecycle on selection change.
310: 	var spawnCmd tea.Cmd
311: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused || selected.Exited {
312: 		// No valid instance (or dead tmux session) — tear down terminal.
313: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
314: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
315: 		}
316: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
317: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
318: 	} else if selected.Title != m.previewTerminalInstance {
319: 		// Different instance selected — swap terminal.
320: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
321: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
322: 		}
323: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
324: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
325: 
326: 		cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
327: 		if cols < 10 {
328: 			cols = 80
329: 		}
330: 		if rows < 5 {
331: 			rows = 24
332: 		}
333: 		capturedTitle := selected.Title
334: 		capturedInstance := selected
335: 		spawnCmd = func() tea.Msg {
336: 			term, err := capturedInstance.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
337: 			return previewTerminalReadyMsg{term: term, instanceTitle: capturedTitle, err: err}
338: 		}
339: 	}
340: 
341: 	m.tabbedWindow.UpdateDiff(selected)
342: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInstance(selected)
343: 	m.updateInfoPane()
344: 	// Update menu with current instance
345: 	m.menu.SetInstance(selected)
346: 
347: 	// Auto-switch tab based on selection type: plan header → info tab, instance → agent tab.
348: 	// Only auto-switch when the nav panel is focused to avoid hijacking explicit tab selection.
349: 	if m.focusSlot == slotNav {
350: 		if selected != nil {
351: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
352: 		} else if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {
353: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
354: 		}
355: 	}
356: 
357: 	// Collect async commands.
358: 	var cmds []tea.Cmd
359: 	if spawnCmd != nil {
360: 		cmds = append(cmds, spawnCmd)
361: 	}
362: 
363: 	if len(cmds) == 0 {
364: 		return nil
365: 	}
366: 	if len(cmds) == 1 {
367: 		return cmds[0]
368: 	}
369: 	return tea.Batch(cmds...)
370: }
371: 
372: // focusInstanceForOverlay selects the given instance in the nav panel and
373: // switches the preview to show its output behind an overlay dialog. The user
374: // can see the agent's terminal behind the overlay to understand context before
375: // responding. Returns a tea.Cmd if an async terminal spawn is needed.
376: func (m *home) focusInstanceForOverlay(inst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
377: 	if inst == nil {
378: 		return nil
379: 	}
380: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
381: 	return m.instanceChanged()
382: }
383: 
384: // focusPlanInstanceForOverlay selects the best instance belonging to the given
385: // plan file, preferring running instances. This is used before showing overlay
386: // dialogs about plan lifecycle events (wave completion, etc.) so the user can
387: // see the agent output behind the overlay.
388: func (m *home) focusPlanInstanceForOverlay(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
389: 	var best *session.Instance
390: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
391: 		if inst.PlanFile != planFile {
392: 			continue
393: 		}
394: 		if best == nil {
395: 			best = inst
396: 		}
397: 		// Prefer running instances over ready/paused ones.
398: 		if inst.Status == session.Running && best.Status != session.Running {
399: 			best = inst
400: 		}
401: 	}
402: 	return m.focusInstanceForOverlay(best)
403: }

405: func statusString(s session.Status) string {
406: 	switch s {
407: 	case session.Running:
408: 		return "running"
409: 	case session.Ready:
410: 		return "ready"
411: 	case session.Loading:
412: 		return "loading"
413: 	case session.Paused:
414: 		return "paused"
415: 	default:
416: 		return "unknown"
417: 	}
418: }

420: // updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader populates the info tab when a plan header is selected
421: // (no instance). Shows plan metadata and instance summary counts.
422: func (m *home) updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader() {
423: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
424: 	if planFile == "" || m.planState == nil {
425: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
426: 		return
427: 	}
428: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(planFile)
429: 	if !ok {
430: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
431: 		return
432: 	}
433: 	data := ui.InfoData{
434: 		IsPlanHeaderSelected: true,
435: 		PlanName:             planstate.DisplayName(planFile),
436: 		PlanStatus:           string(entry.Status),
437: 		PlanTopic:            entry.Topic,
438: 		PlanBranch:           entry.Branch,
439: 	}
440: 	if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
441: 		data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
442: 	}
443: 	// Count instances belonging to this plan.
444: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
445: 		if inst.PlanFile != planFile {
446: 			continue
447: 		}
448: 		data.PlanInstanceCount++
449: 		switch {
450: 		case inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading:
451: 			data.PlanRunningCount++
452: 		case inst.Status == session.Paused:
453: 			data.PlanPausedCount++
454: 		default:
455: 			data.PlanReadyCount++
456: 		}
457: 	}
458: 	// Include wave progress if an orchestrator exists for this plan.
459: 	if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; ok {
460: 		data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
461: 		data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
462: 		tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
463: 		data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
464: 		for i, task := range tasks {
465: 			state := "pending"
466: 			if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
467: 				state = "complete"
468: 			} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
469: 				state = "failed"
470: 			} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
471: 				state = "running"
472: 			}
473: 			data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
474: 		}
475: 	}
476: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
477: }
478: 
479: // updateInfoPane refreshes the info tab data from the selected instance or plan header.
480: func (m *home) updateInfoPane() {
481: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
482: 	if selected == nil {
483: 		// No instance selected — check if a plan header is selected.
484: 		if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {
485: 			m.updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader()
486: 			return
487: 		}
488: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{HasInstance: false})
489: 		return
490: 	}
491: 
492: 	data := ui.InfoData{
493: 		HasInstance: true,
494: 		Title:       selected.Title,
495: 		Program:     selected.Program,
496: 		Branch:      selected.Branch,
497: 		Path:        selected.Path,
498: 		Status:      statusString(selected.Status),
499: 		AgentType:   selected.AgentType,
500: 		TaskNumber:  selected.TaskNumber,
501: 		WaveNumber:  selected.WaveNumber,
502: 	}
503: 
504: 	if !selected.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
505: 		data.Created = selected.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04")
506: 	}
507: 
508: 	if selected.PlanFile != "" {
509: 		if m.planState != nil {
510: 			entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(selected.PlanFile)
511: 			if ok {
512: 				data.HasPlan = true
513: 				data.PlanName = planstate.DisplayName(selected.PlanFile)
514: 				data.PlanDescription = entry.Description
515: 				data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
516: 				data.PlanTopic = entry.Topic
517: 				data.PlanBranch = entry.Branch
518: 				if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
519: 					data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
520: 				}
521: 			}
522: 		}
523: 
524: 		if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[selected.PlanFile]; ok {
525: 			data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
526: 			data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
527: 			tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
528: 			data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
529: 			for i, task := range tasks {
530: 				state := "pending"
531: 				if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
532: 					state = "complete"
533: 				} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
534: 					state = "failed"
535: 				} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
536: 					state = "running"
537: 				}
538: 				data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
539: 			}
540: 		}
541: 	}
542: 
543: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
544: }

546: // loadPlanState reads plan state from the store for the active repo.
547: // Called on user-triggered events (plan creation, repo switch, etc.). The periodic
548: // metadata tick loads plan state in its goroutine instead.
549: // Silently no-ops if the store is not configured.
550: func (m *home) loadPlanState() {
551: 	if m.planStateDir == "" || m.planStore == nil {
552: 		return
553: 	}
554: 	ps, err := planstate.Load(m.planStore, m.planStoreProject, m.planStateDir)
555: 	if err != nil {
556: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not load plan state: %v", err)
557: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
558: 			m.toastManager.Error("plan store error: " + err.Error())
559: 		}
560: 		return
561: 	}
562: 	m.planState = ps
563: }
564: 
565: // updateSidebarPlans pushes the current plans into the sidebar using the three-level tree API.
566: func (m *home) updateSidebarPlans() {
567: 	if m.planState == nil {
568: 		m.nav.SetTopicsAndPlans(nil, nil, nil)
569: 		return
570: 	}
571: 
572: 	// Build topic displays
573: 	topicInfos := m.planState.Topics()
574: 	topics := make([]ui.TopicDisplay, 0, len(topicInfos))
575: 	for _, t := range topicInfos {
576: 		plans := m.planState.PlansByTopic(t.Name)
577: 		planDisplays := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(plans))
578: 		for _, p := range plans {
579: 			if p.Status == planstate.StatusDone || p.Status == planstate.StatusCancelled {
580: 				continue // finished/cancelled plans handled separately
581: 			}
582: 			planDisplays = append(planDisplays, ui.PlanDisplay{
583: 				Filename:    p.Filename,
584: 				Status:      string(p.Status),
585: 				Description: p.Description,
586: 				Branch:      p.Branch,
587: 				Topic:       p.Topic,
588: 			})
589: 		}
590: 		if len(planDisplays) > 0 {
591: 			topics = append(topics, ui.TopicDisplay{Name: t.Name, Plans: planDisplays})
592: 		}
593: 	}
594: 
595: 	// Build ungrouped plans
596: 	ungroupedInfos := m.planState.UngroupedPlans()
597: 	ungrouped := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(ungroupedInfos))
598: 	for _, p := range ungroupedInfos {
599: 		ungrouped = append(ungrouped, ui.PlanDisplay{
600: 			Filename:    p.Filename,
601: 			Status:      string(p.Status),
602: 			Description: p.Description,
603: 			Branch:      p.Branch,
604: 		})
605: 	}
606: 
607: 	// Flatten single-plan topics where topic name matches the plan display name.
608: 	// These don't benefit from a topic header — show the plan directly as ungrouped.
609: 	filtered := topics[:0]
610: 	for _, t := range topics {
611: 		if len(t.Plans) == 1 && t.Name == planstate.DisplayName(t.Plans[0].Filename) {
612: 			ungrouped = append(ungrouped, t.Plans[0])
613: 		} else {
614: 			filtered = append(filtered, t)
615: 		}
616: 	}
617: 	topics = filtered
618: 
619: 	// Build history
620: 	finishedInfos := m.planState.Finished()
621: 	history := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(finishedInfos))
622: 	for _, p := range finishedInfos {
623: 		history = append(history, ui.PlanDisplay{
624: 			Filename:    p.Filename,
625: 			Status:      string(p.Status),
626: 			Description: p.Description,
627: 			Branch:      p.Branch,
628: 			Topic:       p.Topic,
629: 		})
630: 	}
631: 
632: 	// Set plan statuses before the rebuild so navPlanSortKey uses
633: 	// up-to-date running/notification flags in a single pass.
634: 	m.nav.SetPlanStatuses(m.computePlanStatuses())
635: 
636: 	m.nav.SetTopicsAndPlans(topics, ungrouped, history)
637: 
638: }
639: 
640: // checkPlanCompletion scans running coder instances for plans that have been
641: // marked "done" by the agent and, if found, transitions them to reviewer sessions.
642: // Returns a cmd to start the reviewer (may be nil).
643: func (m *home) checkPlanCompletion() tea.Cmd {
644: 	if m.planState == nil {
645: 		return nil
646: 	}
647: 	// Guard: if a reviewer already exists for a plan, do not spawn another.
648: 	// The async metadata tick can overwrite m.planState with a stale snapshot
649: 	// that still shows StatusDone after transitionToReview already ran and set
650: 	// StatusReviewing. Without this guard, a second reviewer is spawned.
651: 	reviewerPlans := make(map[string]bool)
652: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
653: 		if inst.IsReviewer && inst.PlanFile != "" {
654: 			reviewerPlans[inst.PlanFile] = true
655: 		}
656: 	}
657: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
658: 		if inst.PlanFile == "" || inst.IsReviewer {
659: 			continue
660: 		}
661: 		if inst.ImplementationComplete {
662: 			continue // already went through review cycle — don't re-trigger
663: 		}
664: 		if reviewerPlans[inst.PlanFile] {
665: 			continue // reviewer already spawned; skip regardless of stale plan state
666: 		}
667: 		if !m.planState.IsDone(inst.PlanFile) {
668: 			continue
669: 		}
670: 		return m.transitionToReview(inst)
671: 	}
672: 	return nil
673: }
674: 
675: // transitionToReview marks a plan as "reviewing", pauses the coder session,
676: // spawns a reviewer session with the reviewer profile, and returns the start cmd.
677: func (m *home) transitionToReview(coderInst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
678: 	// Guard: transition via FSM before next tick re-reads disk, preventing double-spawn.
679: 	planFile := coderInst.PlanFile
680: 	if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, planfsm.ImplementFinished); err != nil {
681: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not set plan %q to reviewing: %v", planFile, err)
682: 		// Mark complete to break the retry loop — checkPlanCompletion fires
683: 		// every tick and would re-attempt this transition indefinitely.
684: 		coderInst.ImplementationComplete = true
685: 		return nil // FSM rejected — plan is not in implementing state, don't spawn reviewer.
686: 	}
687: 
688: 	// Auto-pause the coder instance — its work is done.
689: 	coderInst.ImplementationComplete = true
690: 	if err := coderInst.Pause(); err != nil {
691: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not pause coder instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
692: 	}
693: 
694: 	return m.spawnReviewer(planFile)
695: }
696: 
697: // spawnReviewer creates and starts a reviewer session for the given plan,
698: // using the plan's shared worktree so it reviews the actual implementation branch.
699: // Does NOT perform any FSM transition — the caller is responsible for that.
700: // Solo agent plans are excluded — the user ends those manually.
701: func (m *home) spawnReviewer(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
702: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
703: 		if inst.PlanFile == planFile && inst.SoloAgent {
704: 			return nil
705: 		}
706: 	}
707: 	planName := planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
708: 	planPath := "docs/plans/" + planFile
709: 	prompt := scaffold.LoadReviewPrompt(planPath, planName)
710: 
711: 	// Kill any previous reviewer for this plan so the new session gets a fresh
712: 	// tmux session instead of reattaching to a stale/errored one.
713: 	m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, session.AgentTypeReviewer)
714: 
715: 	// Resolve the plan's branch for the shared worktree.
716: 	branch := m.planBranch(planFile)
717: 	if branch == "" {
718: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not resolve branch for plan %q", planFile)
719: 		return nil
720: 	}
721: 
722: 	reviewerInst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
723: 		Title:     planName + "-review",
724: 		Path:      m.activeRepoPath,
725: 		Program:   m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeReviewer),
726: 		PlanFile:  planFile,
727: 		AgentType: session.AgentTypeReviewer,
728: 	})
729: 	if err != nil {
730: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not create reviewer instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
731: 		return nil
732: 	}
733: 	reviewerInst.IsReviewer = true
734: 	reviewerInst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
735: 	reviewerInst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
736: 
737: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(reviewerInst, m.nav.AddInstance(reviewerInst))
738: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(reviewerInst) // sort-order safe, unlike index arithmetic
739: 
740: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned reviewer for %s", planName),
741: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
742: 		auditlog.WithInstance(reviewerInst.Title),
743: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeReviewer),
744: 	)
745: 
746: 	m.toastManager.Success(fmt.Sprintf("implementation complete → review started for %s", planName))
747: 
748: 	shared := gitpkg.NewSharedPlanWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
749: 	return func() tea.Msg {
750: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
751: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
752: 		}
753: 		if err := m.materializePlanFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
754: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
755: 		}
756: 		err := reviewerInst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
757: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
758: 	}
759: }

761: func withOpenCodeModelFlag(program, model string) string {
762: 	model = normalizeOpenCodeModelID(model)
763: 	if model == "" {
764: 		return program
765: 	}
766: 
767: 	tokens := strings.Fields(program)
768: 	if len(tokens) == 0 {
769: 		return program
770: 	}
771: 	if filepath.Base(tokens[0]) != "opencode" {
772: 		return program
773: 	}
774: 
775: 	for i, tok := range tokens {
776: 		if tok == "--model" || tok == "-m" {
777: 			if i+1 < len(tokens) {
778: 				return program
779: 			}
780: 			return program
781: 		}
782: 		if strings.HasPrefix(tok, "--model=") {
783: 			return program
784: 		}
785: 	}
786: 
787: 	return program + " --model " + model
788: }
789: 
790: // programForAgent resolves the program command for a given agent type
791: // (e.g. "coder", "planner") using the kasmos config profile. Falls back to
792: // m.program if no profile is configured.
793: //
794: // For typed agents (coder/planner/reviewer), opencode's own --agent flag
795: // handles model selection via its agent config, so we do NOT append --model.
796: // For ad-hoc instances (no agent type), we append --model since there is no
797: // --agent flag to drive model selection.
798: func (m *home) programForAgent(agentType string) string {
799: 	if m.appConfig == nil {
800: 		return m.program
801: 	}
802: 	// Map agent types to config phases or profile names.
803: 	var profile config.AgentProfile
804: 	switch agentType {
805: 	case session.AgentTypeCoder:
806: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("implementing", m.program)
807: 	case session.AgentTypePlanner:
808: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("planning", m.program)
809: 	case session.AgentTypeReviewer:
810: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("quality_review", m.program)
811: 	case session.AgentTypeFixer:
812: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("fixer", m.program)
813: 	default:
814: 		// Ad-hoc — use the "chat" profile if available.
815: 		if p, ok := m.appConfig.Profiles["chat"]; ok && p.Enabled && p.Program != "" {
816: 			profile = p
817: 		} else {
818: 			return m.program
819: 		}
820: 		// Ad-hoc sessions have no --agent flag, so pass --model explicitly.
821: 		prog := profile.BuildCommand()
822: 		return withOpenCodeModelFlag(prog, profile.Model)
823: 	}
824: 	// Typed agents: opencode handles model via --agent <type> + its own config.
825: 	return profile.BuildCommand()
826: }
827: 
828: func normalizeOpenCodeModelID(model string) string {
829: 	model = strings.TrimSpace(model)
830: 	if model == "" || strings.Contains(model, "/") {
831: 		return model
832: 	}
833: 	if strings.HasPrefix(model, "claude-") {
834: 		return "anthropic/" + model
835: 	}
836: 	return model
837: }

839: // killExistingPlanAgent finds and kills any existing instance for the given plan
840: // and agent type, removing it from both the UI list and persistence list.
841: //
842: // IMPORTANT: Instances are removed from both lists BEFORE killing the tmux
843: // session. This prevents the metadata-tick death-detection from seeing a dead
844: // reviewer in the list and auto-firing ReviewApproved (which would prematurely
845: // mark the plan as done).
846: //
847: // For reviewers, also matches legacy instances that only have IsReviewer set.
848: func (m *home) killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, agentType string) {
849: 	// First pass: identify matching instances by title.
850: 	var titles []string
851: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
852: 		if inst.PlanFile != planFile {
853: 			continue
854: 		}
855: 		match := inst.AgentType == agentType
856: 		// Legacy reviewer instances may only have IsReviewer without AgentType.
857: 		if !match && agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer && inst.IsReviewer {
858: 			match = true
859: 		}
860: 		if match {
861: 			titles = append(titles, inst.Title)
862: 		}
863: 	}
864: 
865: 	// Second pass: remove from both lists, then kill tmux.
866: 	// Removal first ensures the death-detection tick cannot see the dead instance.
867: 	for _, title := range titles {
868: 		inst := m.nav.RemoveByTitle(title)
869: 		m.removeFromAllInstances(title)
870: 		if inst != nil {
871: 			if err := inst.Kill(); err != nil {
872: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("could not kill old %s for %q: %v", agentType, planFile, err)
873: 			}
874: 		}
875: 	}
876: }

878: // spawnCoderWithFeedback creates and starts a coder session for the given plan,
879: // injecting reviewer feedback into the implementation prompt. Uses the plan's
880: // shared worktree so fixes are applied to the actual implementation branch.
881: // Does NOT perform any FSM transition — the caller is responsible for that.
882: func (m *home) spawnCoderWithFeedback(planFile, feedback string) tea.Cmd {
883: 	planName := planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
884: 	prompt := buildImplementPrompt(planFile)
885: 	if feedback != "" {
886: 		prompt += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nReviewer feedback from previous round:\n%s", feedback)
887: 	}
888: 
889: 	// Kill any previous coder for this plan so the new session gets a fresh
890: 	// tmux session instead of reattaching to a stale/errored one.
891: 	m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, session.AgentTypeCoder)
892: 
893: 	// Resolve the plan's branch for the shared worktree.
894: 	branch := m.planBranch(planFile)
895: 	if branch == "" {
896: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not resolve branch for plan %q", planFile)
897: 		return nil
898: 	}
899: 
900: 	coderInst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
901: 		Title:     planName + "-implement",
902: 		Path:      m.activeRepoPath,
903: 		Program:   m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeCoder),
904: 		PlanFile:  planFile,
905: 		AgentType: session.AgentTypeCoder,
906: 	})
907: 	if err != nil {
908: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not create coder instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
909: 		return nil
910: 	}
911: 	coderInst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
912: 	coderInst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
913: 
914: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(coderInst, m.nav.AddInstance(coderInst))
915: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(coderInst)
916: 
917: 	detail := ""
918: 	if feedback != "" {
919: 		if len(feedback) > 200 {
920: 			detail = feedback[:200] + "..."
921: 		} else {
922: 			detail = feedback
923: 		}
924: 	}
925: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned coder with reviewer feedback for %s", planName),
926: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
927: 		auditlog.WithInstance(coderInst.Title),
928: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeCoder),
929: 		auditlog.WithDetail(detail),
930: 	)
931: 
932: 	m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("review changes requested → re-implementing %s", planName))
933: 
934: 	shared := gitpkg.NewSharedPlanWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
935: 	return func() tea.Msg {
936: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
937: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
938: 		}
939: 		if err := m.materializePlanFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
940: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
941: 		}
942: 		err := coderInst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
943: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
944: 	}
945: }

947: func (m *home) materializePlanFile(planFile, repoPath string) error {
948: 	if m.planStore == nil {
949: 		return nil
950: 	}
951: 	content, err := m.planStore.GetContent(m.planStoreProject, planFile)
952: 	if err != nil {
953: 		return fmt.Errorf("get plan content %s: %w", planFile, err)
954: 	}
955: 	plansDir := filepath.Join(repoPath, "docs", "plans")
956: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(plansDir, 0o755); err != nil {
957: 		return fmt.Errorf("create plans dir: %w", err)
958: 	}
959: 	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, planFile)
960: 	if err := os.WriteFile(planPath, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
961: 		return fmt.Errorf("write plan file: %w", err)
962: 	}
963: 	return nil
964: }

966: // ingestPlanContent reads the plan markdown file from the given repo path and
967: // stores its content in the DB via the plan store. This bridges the gap between
968: // agents writing plan files to their worktree and the DB being the source of truth.
969: // Called when a PlannerFinished signal is processed.
970: func (m *home) ingestPlanContent(planFile, repoPath string) {
971: 	if m.planStore == nil || m.planState == nil {
972: 		return
973: 	}
974: 	planPath := filepath.Join(repoPath, "docs", "plans", planFile)
975: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(planPath)
976: 	if err != nil {
977: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("ingestPlanContent: cannot read %s: %v", planPath, err)
978: 		return
979: 	}
980: 	if err := m.planState.SetContent(planFile, string(data)); err != nil {
981: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("ingestPlanContent: cannot store content for %s: %v", planFile, err)
982: 	}
983: }

985: // viewSelectedPlan renders the selected plan's markdown in the preview pane.
986: // The rendered output is cached; on cache miss the glamour render runs async
987: // via a tea.Cmd so the UI stays responsive.
988: func (m *home) viewSelectedPlan() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
989: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
990: 	if planFile == "" {
991: 		return m, nil
992: 	}
993: 
994: 	// Cache hit — reuse previously rendered content (instant).
995: 	if planFile == m.cachedPlanFile && m.cachedPlanRendered != "" {
996: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
997: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetDocumentContent(m.cachedPlanRendered)
998: 		return m, nil
999: 	}
1000: 
1001: 	// Cache miss — render async so the UI doesn't freeze.
1002: 	previewWidth, _ := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
1003: 	wordWrap := previewWidth - 4
1004: 	if wordWrap < 40 {
1005: 		wordWrap = 40
1006: 	}
1007: 
1008: 	return m, func() tea.Msg {
1009: 		data, err := m.planStore.GetContent(m.planStoreProject, planFile)
1010: 		if err != nil {
1011: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not read plan %s: %w", planFile, err)}
1012: 		}
1013: 
1014: 		renderer, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
1015: 			glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
1016: 			glamour.WithWordWrap(wordWrap),
1017: 		)
1018: 		if err != nil {
1019: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not create markdown renderer: %w", err)}
1020: 		}
1021: 
1022: 		rendered, err := renderer.Render(data)
1023: 		if err != nil {
1024: 			return planRenderedMsg{err: fmt.Errorf("could not render markdown: %w", err)}
1025: 		}
1026: 
1027: 		return planRenderedMsg{planFile: planFile, rendered: rendered}
1028: 	}
1029: }

1031: // createPlanEntry creates a new plan entry in the store.
1032: func (m *home) createPlanEntry(name, description, topic string) error {
1033: 	if m.planState == nil {
1034: 		if m.planStore == nil {
1035: 			return fmt.Errorf("plan store not configured")
1036: 		}
1037: 		ps, err := planstate.Load(m.planStore, m.planStoreProject, m.planStateDir)
1038: 		if err != nil {
1039: 			return err
1040: 		}
1041: 		m.planState = ps
1042: 	}
1043: 
1044: 	slug := slugifyPlanName(name)
1045: 	filename := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s.md", time.Now().UTC().Format("2006-01-02"), slug)
1046: 	branch := "plan/" + slug
1047: 	if err := m.planState.Create(filename, description, branch, topic, time.Now().UTC()); err != nil {
1048: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
1049: 			m.toastManager.Error("plan store error: " + err.Error())
1050: 		}
1051: 		return err
1052: 	}
1053: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanCreated, "created plan", auditlog.WithPlan(filename))
1054: 	m.updateSidebarPlans()
1055: 	return nil
1056: }

1058: // slugifyPlanName converts a plan name to a URL-safe slug.
1059: func slugifyPlanName(name string) string {
1060: 	name = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
1061: 	name = regexp.MustCompile(`[^a-z0-9]+`).ReplaceAllString(name, "-")
1062: 	return strings.Trim(name, "-")
1063: }
1064: 
1065: // buildPlanFilename derives the plan filename from a human name and creation time.
1066: // "Auth Refactor" → "2026-02-21-auth-refactor.md"
1067: func buildPlanFilename(name string, now time.Time) string {
1068: 	slug := slugifyPlanName(name)
1069: 	if slug == "" {
1070: 		slug = "plan"
1071: 	}
1072: 	return now.UTC().Format("2006-01-02") + "-" + slug + ".md"
1073: }

1075: // renderPlanStub returns the initial markdown content for a new plan file.
1076: func renderPlanStub(name, description, filename string) string {
1077: 	return fmt.Sprintf("# %s\n\n## Context\n\n%s\n\n## Notes\n\n- Created by kas lifecycle flow\n- Plan file: %s\n", name, description, filename)
1078: }

1080: // createPlanRecord registers the plan in the store.
1081: func (m *home) createPlanRecord(planFile, description, branch string, now time.Time) error {
1082: 	if m.planState == nil {
1083: 		if m.planStore == nil {
1084: 			return fmt.Errorf("plan store not configured")
1085: 		}
1086: 		ps, err := planstate.Load(m.planStore, m.planStoreProject, m.planStateDir)
1087: 		if err != nil {
1088: 			return err
1089: 		}
1090: 		m.planState = ps
1091: 	}
1092: 	if err := m.planState.Register(planFile, description, branch, now); err != nil {
1093: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
1094: 			m.toastManager.Error("plan store error: " + err.Error())
1095: 		}
1096: 		return err
1097: 	}
1098: 	return nil
1099: }

1101: // finalizePlanCreation writes the plan stub content to the store, registers it,
1102: // and creates the feature branch. Called at the end of the plan creation wizard.
1103: func (m *home) finalizePlanCreation(name, description string) error {
1104: 	now := time.Now().UTC()
1105: 	planFile := buildPlanFilename(name, now)
1106: 	branch := gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(planFile)
1107: 	content := renderPlanStub(name, description, planFile)
1108: 	if err := m.createPlanRecord(planFile, description, branch, now); err != nil {
1109: 		return err
1110: 	}
1111: 	if err := m.planState.SetContent(planFile, content); err != nil {
1112: 		return err
1113: 	}
1114: 	if err := gitpkg.EnsurePlanBranch(m.activeRepoPath, branch); err != nil {
1115: 		return err
1116: 	}
1117: 
1118: 	m.loadPlanState()
1119: 	m.updateSidebarPlans()
1120: 	return nil
1121: }

1123: func (m *home) importClickUpTask(task *clickup.Task) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1124: 	if task == nil {
1125: 		m.toastManager.Error("clickup fetch failed: empty task payload")
1126: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1127: 	}
1128: 
1129: 	date := time.Now().Format("2006-01-02")
1130: 	filename := clickup.ScaffoldFilename(task.Name, date)
1131: 
1132: 	if m.planState == nil {
1133: 		m.loadPlanState()
1134: 	}
1135: 	if m.planState == nil {
1136: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to register imported plan: plan state unavailable")
1137: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1138: 	}
1139: 
1140: 	filename = dedupePlanFilenameInState(m.planState, filename)
1141: 
1142: 	scaffold := clickup.ScaffoldPlan(*task)
1143: 
1144: 	branch := gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(filename)
1145: 	if err := m.planState.Register(filename, task.Name, branch, time.Now()); err != nil {
1146: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to register imported plan: " + err.Error())
1147: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1148: 	}
1149: 	if err := m.planState.SetContent(filename, scaffold); err != nil {
1150: 		m.toastManager.Error("failed to save imported plan content: " + err.Error())
1151: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1152: 	}
1153: 
1154: 	if err := m.fsm.Transition(filename, planfsm.PlanStart); err != nil {
1155: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("clickup import transition failed for %q: %v", filename, err)
1156: 	}
1157: 
1158: 	m.loadPlanState()
1159: 	m.updateSidebarPlans()
1160: 
1161: 	prompt := fmt.Sprintf(`Analyze this imported ClickUp task. The task details and subtasks are included as reference in the plan file.
1162: 
1163: Determine if the task is well-specified enough for implementation or needs further analysis. Write a proper implementation plan with ## Wave sections, task breakdowns, architecture notes, and tech stack. Use the ClickUp subtasks as a starting point but reorganize into waves based on dependencies.
1164: 
1165: The plan file is at: docs/plans/%s`, filename)
1166: 
1167: 	m.toastManager.Success("imported! spawning planner...")
1168: 	model, cmd := m.spawnPlanAgent(filename, "plan", prompt)
1169: 	if cmd == nil {
1170: 		return model, m.toastTickCmd()
1171: 	}
1172: 	return model, tea.Batch(cmd, m.toastTickCmd())
1173: }
1174: 
1175: func dedupePlanFilename(plansDir, filename string) string {
1176: 	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, filename)
1177: 	if _, err := os.Stat(planPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
1178: 		return filename
1179: 	}
1180: 
1181: 	base := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
1182: 	for i := 2; i < 100; i++ {
1183: 		candidate := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d.md", base, i)
1184: 		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(plansDir, candidate)); os.IsNotExist(err) {
1185: 			return candidate
1186: 		}
1187: 	}
1188: 
1189: 	return filename
1190: }
1191: 
1192: func dedupePlanFilenameInState(ps *planstate.PlanState, filename string) string {
1193: 	if ps == nil {
1194: 		return filename
1195: 	}
1196: 	if _, ok := ps.Entry(filename); !ok {
1197: 		return filename
1198: 	}
1199: 
1200: 	base := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
1201: 	for i := 2; i < 100; i++ {
1202: 		candidate := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d.md", base, i)
1203: 		if _, ok := ps.Entry(candidate); !ok {
1204: 			return candidate
1205: 		}
1206: 	}
1207: 
1208: 	return filename
1209: }
1210: 
1211: // shouldPromptPushAfterCoderExit returns true when a coder session has finished
1212: // and the plan is still in the implementing state. A coder is considered finished
1213: // when its tmux session has exited (tmuxAlive == false) OR when it has returned
1214: // to a prompt after completing its queued work (PromptDetected && !AwaitingWork).
1215: // The latter covers "applying fixes" coders spawned by spawnCoderWithFeedback,
1216: // which finish their work and return to prompt rather than exiting tmux.
1217: func shouldPromptPushAfterCoderExit(entry planstate.PlanEntry, inst *session.Instance, tmuxAlive bool) bool {
1218: 	if inst == nil {
1219: 		return false
1220: 	}
1221: 	if inst.PlanFile == "" {
1222: 		return false
1223: 	}
1224: 	if inst.AgentType != session.AgentTypeCoder {
1225: 		return false
1226: 	}
1227: 	if inst.SoloAgent {
1228: 		return false
1229: 	}
1230: 	if entry.Status != planstate.StatusImplementing {
1231: 		return false
1232: 	}
1233: 	// Tmux exited — original single-coder completion path.
1234: 	if !tmuxAlive {
1235: 		return true
1236: 	}
1237: 	// Agent returned to prompt after finishing queued work — covers the
1238: 	// review-feedback coder ("applying fixes") which stays alive in tmux.
1239: 	if inst.PromptDetected && !inst.AwaitingWork {
1240: 		return true
1241: 	}
1242: 	return false
1243: }
1244: 
1245: // promptPushBranchThenAdvance shows a confirmation overlay asking the user to
1246: // push the implementation branch, then advances the plan to reviewing and
1247: // spawns a reviewer agent via coderCompleteMsg.
1248: func (m *home) promptPushBranchThenAdvance(inst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
1249: 	capturedPlanFile := inst.PlanFile
1250: 	capturedTitle := inst.Title
1251: 	message := fmt.Sprintf("[!] implementation finished for '%s'. push branch now?", planstate.DisplayName(capturedPlanFile))
1252: 	pushAction := func() tea.Msg {
1253: 		worktree, err := inst.GetGitWorktree()
1254: 		if err == nil {
1255: 			_ = worktree.PushChanges(
1256: 				fmt.Sprintf("[kas] push completed implementation for '%s'", capturedTitle),
1257: 				false,
1258: 			)
1259: 		}
1260: 		return coderCompleteMsg{planFile: capturedPlanFile}
1261: 	}
1262: 	return m.confirmAction(message, func() tea.Msg { return pushAction() })
1263: }
1264: 
1265: // planBranch resolves the branch name for a plan, backfilling if needed.
1266: func (m *home) planBranch(planFile string) string {
1267: 	if m.planState == nil {
1268: 		return ""
1269: 	}
1270: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(planFile)
1271: 	if !ok {
1272: 		return ""
1273: 	}
1274: 	if entry.Branch == "" {
1275: 		entry.Branch = gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(planFile)
1276: 		_ = m.planState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch)
1277: 	}
1278: 	return entry.Branch
1279: }
