1281: // buildPlanPrompt returns the initial prompt for a planner agent session.
1282: // The prompt explicitly requires ## Wave N headers because kasmos uses them
1283: // for wave orchestration — without them, implementation cannot start.
1284: func buildPlanPrompt(planName, description string) string {
1285: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1286: 		"Plan %s. Goal: %s. "+
1287: 			"Use the `kasmos-planner` skill. "+
1288: 			"The plan MUST include ## Wave N sections (at minimum ## Wave 1) "+
1289: 			"grouping all tasks — kasmos requires Wave headers to orchestrate implementation.",
1290: 		planName, description,
1291: 	)
1292: }
1293: 
1294: // buildWaveAnnotationPrompt returns the prompt used when a planner is respawned
1295: // to add ## Wave headers to an existing plan that is missing them.
1296: // It instructs the planner to annotate the plan, commit the change, and write
1297: // the sentinel signal so kasmos can resume the implementation flow.
1298: func buildWaveAnnotationPrompt(planFile string) string {
1299: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1300: 		"The plan at docs/plans/%[1]s is missing ## Wave N headers required for kasmos wave orchestration. "+
1301: 			"Please annotate the plan by wrapping all tasks under ## Wave N sections. "+
1302: 			"Every plan needs at least ## Wave 1 — even single-task trivial plans. "+
1303: 			"Keep all existing task content intact; only add the ## Wave headers.\n\n"+
1304: 			"After annotating:\n"+
1305: 			"1. Commit: git add docs/plans/%[1]s && git commit -m \"plan: add wave headers to %[1]s\"\n"+
1306: 			"2. Signal completion: touch .kasmos/signals/planner-finished-%[1]s\n"+
1307: 			"Do not edit plan-state.json directly.",
1308: 		planFile,
1309: 	)
1310: }
1311: 
1312: // buildImplementPrompt returns the prompt for a coder agent session.
1313: // The plan file is materialized to docs/plans/<planFile> in the agent's worktree
1314: // by the TUI before the agent starts. Agents write sentinel signals to
1315: // .kasmos/signals/ in their worktree; the TUI ingests them on completion.
1316: func buildImplementPrompt(planFile string) string {
1317: 	return fmt.Sprintf(
1318: 		"Implement docs/plans/%s using the `kasmos-coder` skill. Execute all tasks sequentially.",
1319: 		planFile,
1320: 	)
1321: }
1322: 
1323: // buildSoloPrompt returns a minimal prompt for a solo agent session.
1324: // If planFile is non-empty, it references the plan file. Otherwise just name + description.
1325: func buildSoloPrompt(planName, description, planFile string) string {
1326: 	if planFile != "" {
1327: 		return fmt.Sprintf("Implement %s. Goal: %s. Plan: docs/plans/%s", planName, description, planFile)
1328: 	}
1329: 	return fmt.Sprintf("Implement %s. Goal: %s.", planName, description)
1330: }
1331: 
1332: // buildModifyPlanPrompt returns the prompt for modifying an existing plan.
1333: func buildModifyPlanPrompt(planFile string) string {
1334: 	return fmt.Sprintf("Modify existing plan at docs/plans/%s. Keep the same filename and update only what changed.", planFile)
1335: }
1336: 
1337: // agentTypeForSubItem maps a sidebar stage name to the corresponding AgentType constant.
1338: func agentTypeForSubItem(action string) (string, bool) {
1339: 	switch action {
1340: 	case "plan":
1341: 		return session.AgentTypePlanner, true
1342: 	case "implement", "solo":
1343: 		return session.AgentTypeCoder, true
1344: 	case "review":
1345: 		return session.AgentTypeReviewer, true
1346: 	default:
1347: 		return "", false
1348: 	}
1349: }
1350: 
1351: // spawnAdHocAgent creates and starts an ad-hoc agent session (no plan, no lifecycle).
1352: // branch and workPath are optional overrides - empty strings use defaults.
1353: func (m *home) spawnAdHocAgent(name, branch, workPath string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1354: 	path := m.activeRepoPath
1355: 	if workPath != "" {
1356: 		path = workPath
1357: 	}
1358: 
1359: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
1360: 		Title:   name,
1361: 		Path:    path,
1362: 		Program: m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeFixer),
1363: 	})
1364: 	if err != nil {
1365: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1366: 	}
1367: 
1368: 	inst.AgentType = session.AgentTypeFixer
1369: 	inst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
1370: 	inst.LoadingTotal = 8
1371: 	inst.LoadingMessage = "preparing session..."
1372: 
1373: 	m.state = stateDefault
1374: 	m.menu.SetState(ui.StateDefault)
1375: 
1376: 	var startCmd tea.Cmd
1377: 	switch {
1378: 	case workPath != "" && branch == "":
1379: 		// Path override only - run in-place on main branch (no worktree)
1380: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1381: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnMainBranch()}
1382: 		}
1383: 
1384: 	case branch != "":
1385: 		// Branch override - create worktree on specified branch
1386: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1387: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnBranch(branch)}
1388: 		}
1389: 
1390: 	default:
1391: 		// No overrides - run in-place on current branch (no worktree)
1392: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1393: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnMainBranch()}
1394: 		}
1395: 	}
1396: 
1397: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned fixer agent: %s", name),
1398: 		auditlog.WithInstance(name),
1399: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeFixer),
1400: 	)
1401: 
1402: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(inst, m.nav.AddInstance(inst))
1403: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
1404: 	return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), startCmd)
1405: }
1406: 
1407: // spawnPlanAgent creates and starts an agent session for the given plan and action.
1408: func (m *home) spawnPlanAgent(planFile, action, prompt string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1409: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(planFile)
1410: 	if !ok {
1411: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", planFile))
1412: 	}
1413: 
1414: 	agentType, ok := agentTypeForSubItem(action)
1415: 	if !ok {
1416: 		return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("unknown plan action: %s", action))
1417: 	}
1418: 
1419: 	// Kill any existing instance of the same type for this plan to prevent
1420: 	// duplicates (e.g. user triggers "start review" when a reviewer already exists).
1421: 	if agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer || agentType == session.AgentTypePlanner {
1422: 		m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, agentType)
1423: 	}
1424: 
1425: 	title := planstate.DisplayName(planFile) + "-" + action
1426: 	if action == "solo" {
1427: 		// Solo sessions run on main branch, so their tmux session names must stay
1428: 		// unique to avoid accidentally reattaching to another solo session.
1429: 		title = planstate.DisplayName(planFile) + "-solo"
1430: 	}
1431: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
1432: 		Title:     title,
1433: 		Path:      m.activeRepoPath,
1434: 		Program:   m.programForAgent(agentType),
1435: 		PlanFile:  planFile,
1436: 		AgentType: agentType,
1437: 	})
1438: 	if err != nil {
1439: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1440: 	}
1441: 	// Keep IsReviewer in sync with AgentType so the reviewer-completion check
1442: 	// in the metadata tick handler (which gates on inst.IsReviewer) fires for
1443: 	// sidebar-spawned reviewers as well as auto-spawned ones.
1444: 	if agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer {
1445: 		inst.IsReviewer = true
1446: 	}
1447: 	if action == "solo" {
1448: 		inst.SoloAgent = true
1449: 	}
1450: 	inst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
1451: 
1452: 	// Set loading state immediately so the UI shows the progress bar
1453: 	// instead of the idle banner while the async start runs.
1454: 	inst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
1455: 	inst.LoadingTotal = 5
1456: 	inst.LoadingMessage = "Preparing session..."
1457: 
1458: 	var startCmd tea.Cmd
1459: 	if action == "plan" || action == "solo" {
1460: 		// Planner and solo agent run on main branch — no worktree created.
1461: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1462: 			if err := m.materializePlanFile(planFile, m.activeRepoPath); err != nil {
1463: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1464: 			}
1465: 			err := inst.StartOnMainBranch()
1466: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1467: 		}
1468: 	} else {
1469: 		// Backfill branch name for plans created before the branch field was introduced.
1470: 		if entry.Branch == "" {
1471: 			entry.Branch = gitpkg.PlanBranchFromFile(planFile)
1472: 			if err := m.planState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch); err != nil {
1473: 				return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("failed to assign branch for plan: %w", err))
1474: 			}
1475: 		}
1476: 
1477: 		// Coder and reviewer share the plan's feature branch worktree
1478: 		shared := gitpkg.NewSharedPlanWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, entry.Branch)
1479: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
1480: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
1481: 		}
1482: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1483: 			if err := m.materializePlanFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
1484: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1485: 			}
1486: 			err := inst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, entry.Branch)
1487: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1488: 		}
1489: 	}
1490: 
1491: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned %s for plan %s", agentType, planstate.DisplayName(planFile)),
1492: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
1493: 		auditlog.WithInstance(title),
1494: 		auditlog.WithAgent(agentType),
1495: 	)
1496: 
1497: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(inst, m.nav.AddInstance(inst))
1498: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
1499: 	return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), startCmd)
1500: }
1501: 
1502: // getTopicNames returns existing topic names for the picker.
1503: func (m *home) getTopicNames() []string {
1504: 	if m.planState == nil {
1505: 		return nil
1506: 	}
1507: 	topics := m.planState.Topics()
1508: 	names := make([]string, len(topics))
1509: 	for i, t := range topics {
1510: 		names[i] = t.Name
1511: 	}
1512: 	return names
1513: }
1514: 
1515: // rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators reconstructs in-memory WaveOrchestrators for plans that
1516: // were mid-wave when kasmos was restarted. Without this, the wave completion monitor and
1517: // the "Mark complete" context menu action are both inoperative after a restart.
1518: //
1519: // For each plan that is StatusImplementing and has task instances (TaskNumber > 0) but
1520: // no orchestrator, we:
1521: //  1. Parse the plan file to get the wave/task structure.
1522: //  2. Fast-forward the orchestrator to the wave the instances are on.
1523: //  3. Mark tasks as complete for instances that are already paused (finished their work).
1524: //
1525: // Tasks that are still running remain in taskRunning state so the metadata tick can
1526: // detect their completion normally (or the user can mark them complete manually).
1527: func (m *home) rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators() {
1528: 	if m.planState == nil || m.planStateDir == "" {
1529: 		return
1530: 	}
1531: 
1532: 	// Group task instances by plan file.
1533: 	type taskInst struct {
1534: 		taskNumber int
1535: 		waveNumber int
1536: 		paused     bool
1537: 	}
1538: 	byPlan := make(map[string][]taskInst)
1539: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
1540: 		if inst.TaskNumber == 0 || inst.PlanFile == "" {
1541: 			continue
1542: 		}
1543: 		byPlan[inst.PlanFile] = append(byPlan[inst.PlanFile], taskInst{
1544: 			taskNumber: inst.TaskNumber,
1545: 			waveNumber: inst.WaveNumber,
1546: 			paused:     inst.Paused(),
1547: 		})
1548: 	}
1549: 
1550: 	for planFile, tasks := range byPlan {
1551: 		// Skip if orchestrator already exists.
1552: 		if _, exists := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; exists {
1553: 			continue
1554: 		}

Now I have a clear picture of how the codebase works. Let me understand the current structure:

1. **Context menu for instances** (`openContextMenu()` in `app_actions.go`): Shows items like "open", "kill", "pause"/"resume", "focus agent", "rename", "push branch", "create pr".

2. **`executeContextAction`**: Handles each action string.

3. The key question is: how do we restart an agent? 

For **plan-bound agents** (coder, reviewer, planner):
- Kill the current tmux session
- The instance has a `QueuedPrompt` and `AgentType`


- Need to respawn with the same config and prompt

For **ad-hoc agents** (no plan):
- Kill the current tmux session
- Re-spawn with the same path/program/branch

The instance structure gives me everything I need: `Title`, `Path`, `Program`, `PlanFile`, `AgentType`, `Branch`, and `QueuedPrompt` to reconstruct the agent exactly as it was.

The key distinction is that `Kill()` terminates both tmux and the git worktree, while `StopTmux()` only kills the session and leaves the worktree intact. For a restart, I should use `StopTmux()` to preserve the existing workspace, then re-launch a fresh tmux session with the same configuration.

Looking at `Resume()`, it recreates the worktree and restarts tmux, but for restart I need to keep the same worktree. The user wants to kill the existing session and launch a fresh agent with identical configuration and prompt. The challenge is retrieving the original prompt since `QueuedPrompt` gets cleared after being sent—though for plan-bound instances, I could reconstruct it from the plan file.

For plan-bound agents, I'd kill the tmux session while preserving the worktree, then start a new session and resend the reconstructed prompt. For ad-hoc agents, it's simpler: just kill and restart the session in the same worktree without a specific prompt. I'm noticing that after `Kill()`, the `started` flag isn't reset, which might affect how the lifecycle methods behave on restart.

Looking at the actual implementation, I need to stop just the tmux session while keeping the worktree intact, reset the instance state to allow relaunching, and then start a fresh tmux session. The key insight is that `StopTmux()` closes the session, and then I can call `tmuxSession.Start()` again with the worktree path—similar to how `Resume()` handles this after the worktree is already set up.

For the `Restart` method, I need to kill the existing tmux session first, recreate it with the same worktree, and queue up the original prompt to be sent again. But I also need to account for the different agent types: planners run on the main branch without a dedicated worktree, while coders and reviewers use a shared worktree.

The key insight is that I can check the `gitWorktree` field to determine whether the instance was running on main branch or in a worktree, then restart accordingly. Let me implement the `Restart` method that handles both cases. the tmux session and create a new one in the same location, then reconstruct the appropriate prompt based on the agent type—using the plan prompt for planners, the implement prompt for coders, the review prompt for reviewers, or no prompt for solo agents and ad-hoc fixes.

Now I'm implementing the `Restart()` method...

If a prompt is provided, I'll queue it and transfer it to the CLI, then restart the tmux session in the appropriate directory—either the git worktree path if one exists, or the instance's base path. After that, I need to reset all the state flags to their initial values and set the status back to Running.

Now I'm thinking through the restart logic more carefully—I need to make sure the instance was actually started before attempting to restart, and I'm realizing the flow mirrors what happens in `StartOnMainBranch()` and `StartInSharedWorktree()`: setting up the tmux session, configuring the environment and title, transferring the prompt, then starting the session. The key difference for restart is that we're reusing the existing worktree rather than creating a new one. I should also check how the `started` flag is used elsewhere—it appears in `Kill()`, `StopTmux()`, `Pause()`, and `Resume()` to determine whether cleanup or state changes are needed.

For the implementation approach, I'm deciding between putting the restart logic directly on `Instance` or handling it at the app layer. Since restart involves both low-level tmux operations and app-specific concerns like reconstructing the prompt and determining the correct path, I think the cleanest split is to add a `RestartTmux` method on `Instance` that handles the session lifecycle, while keeping the prompt reconstruction and path logic in `executeContextAction`.

Adding the restart method... Continuing with the restart logic, I need to determine the working directory based on whether a git worktree exists, then start the tmux session from that location. After that, I'm resetting all the run-state flags to their initial values and setting the status back to Running before returning.

Now I'm adding the restart action to the context menu by including it in the menu items and then handling the `"restart_instance"` case in the action executor. When restart is triggered, I'm pulling the selected instance and reconstructing the appropriate prompt based on its plan file and agent type.

1415: 		return m.startNextWave(orch, msg.entry)
1416: 	case waveRetryMsg:
1417: 		orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[msg.planFile]
1418: 		if !ok {
1419: 			return m, nil
1420: 		}
1421: 		return m.retryFailedWaveTasks(orch, msg.entry)
1422: 	case waveAbortMsg:
1423: 		delete(m.waveOrchestrators, msg.planFile)
1424: 		// Kill and remove all task instances that belong to the aborted plan.
1425: 		// Their tmux sessions are already dead (tasks failed), so no worktree
1426: 		// check is needed — just clean them out of the list.
1427: 		// Collect first to avoid mutating m.allInstances while iterating it.
1428: 		var taskInsts []*session.Instance
1429: 		for _, inst := range m.allInstances {
1430: 			if inst.PlanFile == msg.planFile && inst.TaskNumber > 0 {
1431: 				taskInsts = append(taskInsts, inst)
1432: 			}
1433: 		}
1434: 		for _, inst := range taskInsts {
1435: 			if m.nav.SelectInstance(inst) {
1436: 				m.nav.Kill()
1437: 			}
1438: 			m.removeFromAllInstances(inst.Title)
1439: 		}
1440: 		m.saveAllInstances()
1441: 		m.updateNavPanelStatus()
1442: 		m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("wave orchestration aborted for %s",
1443: 			planstate.DisplayName(msg.planFile)))
1444: 		return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), m.instanceChanged(), m.toastTickCmd())
1445: 	case waveAllCompleteMsg:
1446: 		// All waves finished and user confirmed — push branch and advance to review.
1447: 		planFile := msg.planFile
1448: 		planName := planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
1449: 
1450: 		// Push the implementation branch (best-effort, non-blocking).
1451: 		for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
1452: 			if inst.PlanFile == planFile && inst.TaskNumber > 0 {
1453: 				worktree, err := inst.GetGitWorktree()
1454: 				if err == nil {
1455: 					_ = worktree.PushChanges(
1456: 						fmt.Sprintf("[kas] push completed implementation for '%s'", planName),
1457: 						false,
1458: 					)
1459: 					break // one push is enough — all tasks share the worktree
1460: 				}
1461: 			}
1462: 		}
1463: 
1464: 		// Transition FSM implementing → reviewing.
1465: 		if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, planfsm.ImplementFinished); err != nil {
1466: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("wave all-complete: could not transition %q to reviewing: %v", planFile, err)
1467: 		}
1468: 		m.loadPlanState()
1469: 		m.updateSidebarPlans()
1470: 
1471: 		// Spawn reviewer agent for the completed plan.
1472: 		var reviewerCmd tea.Cmd
1473: 		if cmd := m.spawnReviewer(planFile); cmd != nil {
1474: 			reviewerCmd = cmd
1475: 		}
1476: 		m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("all waves complete for '%s' — starting review", planName))
1477: 		return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), reviewerCmd, m.toastTickCmd())
1478: 	case coderCompleteMsg:
1479: 		// Single-coder (non-wave) implementation finished and user confirmed push.
1480: 		// Transition FSM and spawn reviewer — mirrors waveAllCompleteMsg flow.
1481: 		planFile := msg.planFile
1482: 		planName := planstate.DisplayName(planFile)
1483: 
1484: 		if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, planfsm.ImplementFinished); err != nil {
1485: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("coder-complete: could not transition %q to reviewing: %v", planFile, err)
1486: 		}
1487: 
1488: 		// Mark the coder instance as implementation-complete and pause it.
1489: 		for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
1490: 			if inst.PlanFile == planFile && inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypeCoder {
1491: 				inst.ImplementationComplete = true
1492: 				_ = inst.Pause()
1493: 				break
1494: 			}
1495: 		}
1496: 
1497: 		m.loadPlanState()
1498: 		m.updateSidebarPlans()
1499: 
1500: 		var reviewerCmd tea.Cmd
1501: 		if cmd := m.spawnReviewer(planFile); cmd != nil {
1502: 			reviewerCmd = cmd
1503: 		}
1504: 		m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("implementation complete for '%s' — starting review", planName))
1505: 		return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), reviewerCmd, m.toastTickCmd())
1506: 	case tmuxSessionsMsg:
1507: 		if msg.err != nil {
1508: 			return m, m.handleError(msg.err)
1509: 		}
1510: 		if len(msg.sessions) == 0 {
1511: 			if m.toastManager != nil {
1512: 				m.toastManager.Info("no kas tmux sessions found")
1513: 				return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1514: 			}
1515: 			return m, nil
1516: 		}
1517: 		// Build instance lookup for enrichment.
1518: 		instMap := make(map[string]*session.Instance, len(m.allInstances))
1519: 		for _, inst := range m.allInstances {
1520: 			if inst.Started() {
1521: 				instMap[tmux.ToKasTmuxNamePublic(inst.Title)] = inst
1522: 			}
1523: 		}
1524: 		items := make([]overlay.TmuxBrowserItem, len(msg.sessions))
1525: 		for i, s := range msg.sessions {
1526: 			items[i] = overlay.TmuxBrowserItem{
1527: 				Name:     s.Name,
1528: 				Title:    s.Title,
1529: 				Created:  s.Created,
1530: 				Windows:  s.Windows,
1531: 				Attached: s.Attached,
1532: 				Width:    s.Width,
1533: 				Height:   s.Height,
1534: 				Managed:  s.Managed,
1535: 			}
1536: 			if inst, ok := instMap[s.Name]; ok {
1537: 				items[i].PlanFile = inst.PlanFile
1538: 				items[i].AgentType = inst.AgentType
1539: 				items[i].Status = statusString(inst.Status)
1540: 			}
1541: 		}
1542: 		m.tmuxBrowser = overlay.NewTmuxBrowserOverlay(items)
1543: 		m.state = stateTmuxBrowser
1544: 		return m, nil
1545: 	case tmuxKillResultMsg:
1546: 		if msg.err != nil {
1547: 			m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("failed to kill session: %v", msg.err))
1548: 		} else {
1549: 			m.toastManager.Success(fmt.Sprintf("killed session '%s'", msg.name))
1550: 		}
1551: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
1552: 	case tmuxAttachReturnMsg:
1553: 		m.toastManager.Info("detached from tmux session")
1554: 		return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), m.toastTickCmd())
1555: 	case permissionAutoApproveMsg:
1556: 		if msg.instance != nil && msg.instance.Started() {
1557: 			i := msg.instance
1558: 			return m, func() tea.Msg {
1559: 				i.SendPermissionResponse(tmux.PermissionAllowAlways)
1560: 				return nil
1561: 			}
1562: 		}
1563: 		return m, nil
1564: 	case planTitleMsg:
1565: 		if m.state == stateNewPlanDeriving {
1566: 			if msg.err == nil && msg.title != "" {
1567: 				m.pendingPlanName = msg.title
1568: 			}
1569: 			topicNames := m.getTopicNames()
1570: 			topicNames = append([]string{"(No topic)"}, topicNames...)
1571: 			pickerTitle := fmt.Sprintf("assign to topic for '%s'", m.pendingPlanName)
1572: 			m.pickerOverlay = overlay.NewPickerOverlay(pickerTitle, topicNames)
1573: 			m.pickerOverlay.SetAllowCustom(true)
1574: 			m.state = stateNewPlanTopic
1575: 			return m, nil
1576: 		}
1577: 		// Safety net: if title arrives while already in topic picker, update silently
1578: 		if msg.err == nil && msg.title != "" {
1579: 			if m.state == stateNewPlanTopic && m.pendingPlanDesc != "" {
1580: 				m.pendingPlanName = msg.title
1581: 				if m.pickerOverlay != nil {
1582: 					m.pickerOverlay.SetTitle(
1583: 						fmt.Sprintf("assign to topic for '%s'", msg.title),
1584: 					)
1585: 					return m, tea.WindowSize()
1586: 				}
1587: 			}
1588: 		}
1589: 		return m, nil
1590: 	case plannerCompleteMsg:
1591: 		// User confirmed: start implementation. Kill the planner instance (may still be alive
1592: 		// when triggered by the PlannerFinished sentinel, unlike the tmux-death path).
1593: 		m.plannerPrompted[msg.planFile] = true
1594: 		m.killExistingPlanAgent(msg.planFile, session.AgentTypePlanner)
1595: 		_ = m.saveAllInstances()
1596: 		m.pendingPlannerInstanceTitle = ""
1597: 		m.pendingPlannerPlanFile = ""
1598: 		m.updateNavPanelStatus()
1599: 		return m.triggerPlanStage(msg.planFile, "implement")
1600: 	case instanceStartedMsg:
1601: 		if msg.err != nil {
1602: 			// Remove the specific instance that failed — not the currently selected one.
1603: 			_ = msg.instance.Kill()
1604: 			m.nav.RemoveByTitle(msg.instance.Title)
1605: 			m.removeFromAllInstances(msg.instance.Title)
1606: 			m.updateNavPanelStatus()
1607: 			return m, m.handleError(msg.err)
1608: 		}
1609: 		// Instance started successfully — add to master list, save and finalize
1610: 		m.allInstances = append(m.allInstances, msg.instance)
1611: 		if err := m.saveAllInstances(); err != nil {
1612: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
1613: 		}
1614: 		m.updateNavPanelStatus()
1615: 		if fn, ok := m.instanceFinalizers[msg.instance]; ok {
1616: 			fn()
1617: 			delete(m.instanceFinalizers, msg.instance)
1618: 		}
1619: 		if m.autoYes {
1620: 			msg.instance.AutoYes = true
1621: 		}
1622: 		return m, tea.Batch(tea.WindowSize(), m.instanceChanged())
1623: 	case spinner.TickMsg:
1624: 		var cmd tea.Cmd
1625: 		m.spinner, cmd = m.spinner.Update(msg)
1626: 		return m, cmd
1627: 	default:
1628: 		// Forward unknown CSI sequences (kitty keyboard protocol, etc.) to the
1629: 		// embedded PTY when in focus/interactive mode. Bubbletea emits these as
1630: 		// an unexported []byte-based type; use reflect to extract raw bytes.
1631: 		if m.state == stateFocusAgent && m.previewTerminal != nil {
1632: 			v := reflect.ValueOf(msg)
1633: 			if v.Kind() == reflect.Slice && v.Type().Elem().Kind() == reflect.Uint8 {
1634: 				if data := v.Bytes(); len(data) > 0 {
1635: 					_ = m.previewTerminal.SendKey(data)
1636: 				}
1637: 				return m, nil
1638: 			}
1639: 		}
1640: 	}
1641: 	return m, nil
1642: }
1643: 
1644: func (m *home) handleQuit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
1645: 	// Check if any instances are actively running or loading.
1646: 	hasActive := false
1647: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
1648: 		if inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading {
1649: 			hasActive = true
1650: 			break
1651: 		}
1652: 	}
1653: 
1654: 	if hasActive {
1655: 		quitAction := func() tea.Msg {
1656: 			m.audit(auditlog.EventSessionStopped, "kasmos stopped")
1657: 			_ = m.saveAllInstances()
1658: 			return tea.QuitMsg{}
1659: 		}
1660: 		return m, m.confirmAction("quit kasmos? active sessions will be preserved.", quitAction)
1661: 	}
1662: 
1663: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventSessionStopped, "kasmos stopped")
1664: 	if err := m.saveAllInstances(); err != nil {
1665: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1666: 	}
1667: 	return m, tea.Quit
1668: }

1670: func (m *home) View() string {
1671: 	// All columns use identical padding and height for uniform alignment.
1672: 	colStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().Height(m.contentHeight)
1673: 	previewWithPadding := colStyle.Render(m.tabbedWindow.String())
1674: 
1675: 	// Layout: nav | preview/tabs
1676: 	var cols []string
1677: 	if !m.sidebarHidden {
1678: 		cols = append(cols, colStyle.Render(m.nav.String()))
1679: 	}
1680: 	cols = append(cols, previewWithPadding)
1681: 	listAndPreview := lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Top, cols...)
1682: 	statusBarView := ""
1683: 	if m.statusBar != nil {
1684: 		m.statusBar.SetData(m.computeStatusBarData())
1685: 		statusBarView = m.statusBar.String()
1686: 	}
1687: 	if m.menu != nil && m.nav != nil {
1688: 		m.menu.SetSidebarSpaceAction(m.nav.SelectedSpaceAction())
1689: 	}
1690: 
1691: 	mainView := lipgloss.JoinVertical(
1692: 		lipgloss.Left,
1693: 		statusBarView,
1694: 		listAndPreview,
1695: 		m.menu.String(),
1696: 	)
1697: 
1698: 	var result string
1699: 	switch {
1700: 	case m.state == stateSendPrompt && m.textInputOverlay != nil:
1701: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1702: 	case m.state == statePRTitle && m.textInputOverlay != nil:
1703: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1704: 	case m.state == statePRBody && m.textInputOverlay != nil:
1705: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1706: 	case m.state == stateRenameInstance && m.textInputOverlay != nil:
1707: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1708: 	case m.state == stateRenamePlan && m.textInputOverlay != nil:
1709: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1710: 	case m.state == stateNewPlan && m.textInputOverlay != nil:
1711: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1712: 	case m.state == stateSpawnAgent && m.formOverlay != nil:
1713: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.formOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1714: 	case m.state == stateNewPlanTopic && m.pickerOverlay != nil:
1715: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.pickerOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1716: 	case m.state == stateClickUpSearch && m.textInputOverlay != nil:
1717: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1718: 	case m.state == stateClickUpPicker && m.pickerOverlay != nil:
1719: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.pickerOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1720: 	case m.state == stateClickUpWorkspacePicker && m.pickerOverlay != nil:
1721: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.pickerOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1722: 	case m.state == stateChangeTopic && m.pickerOverlay != nil:
1723: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.pickerOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1724: 	case m.state == stateSetStatus && m.pickerOverlay != nil:
1725: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.pickerOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1726: 	case m.state == statePrompt:
1727: 		if m.textInputOverlay == nil {
1728: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("text input overlay is nil")
1729: 		}
1730: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1731: 	case m.state == stateHelp:
1732: 		if m.textOverlay == nil {
1733: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("text overlay is nil")
1734: 		}
1735: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1736: 	case m.state == stateConfirm:
1737: 		if m.confirmationOverlay == nil {
1738: 			log.ErrorLog.Printf("confirmation overlay is nil")
1739: 		}
1740: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.confirmationOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1741: 	case m.state == statePermission && m.permissionOverlay != nil:
1742: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.permissionOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1743: 	case m.state == stateContextMenu && m.contextMenu != nil:
1744: 		cx, cy := m.contextMenu.GetPosition()
1745: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(cx, cy, m.contextMenu.Render(), mainView, true, false)
1746: 	case m.state == stateChatAboutPlan && m.textInputOverlay != nil:
1747: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.textInputOverlay.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1748: 	case m.state == stateTmuxBrowser && m.tmuxBrowser != nil:
1749: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(0, 0, m.tmuxBrowser.Render(), mainView, true, true)
1750: 	default:
1751: 		result = mainView
1752: 	}
1753: 
1754: 	if toastView := m.toastManager.View(); toastView != "" {
1755: 		x, y := m.toastManager.GetPosition()
1756: 		result = overlay.PlaceOverlay(x, y, toastView, result, false, false)
1757: 	}
1758: 
1759: 	// Process bubblezone markers before rendering is complete
1760: 	// (zone markers inflate lipgloss.Width if left in place).
1761: 	result = safeZoneScan(result)
1762: 
1763: 	// Height-fill — ensure enough lines for bubbletea's alt-screen renderer.
1764: 	// OSC 11 handles the actual background color; this just pads vertically.
1765: 	result = ui.FillBackground(result, m.termHeight)
1766: 
1767: 	return result
1768: }
1769: 
1770: func safeZoneScan(s string) (scanned string) {
1771: 	defer func() {
1772: 		if recover() != nil {
1773: 			scanned = s
1774: 		}
1775: 	}()
1776: 
1777: 	return zone.Scan(s)
1778: }

1933: 	Updated            bool
1934: 	HasPrompt          bool
1935: 	DiffStats          *git.DiffStats
1936: 	CPUPercent         float64
1937: 	MemMB              float64
1938: 	ResourceUsageValid bool
1939: 	TmuxAlive          bool
1940: 	PermissionPrompt   *session.PermissionPrompt // non-nil when opencode shows a permission dialog
1941: }
1942: 
1943: // metadataResultMsg carries all per-instance metadata collected by the async tick.
1944: type metadataResultMsg struct {
1945: 	Results          []instanceMetadata
1946: 	PlanState        *planstate.PlanState // pre-loaded plan state (nil if dir not set)
1947: 	Signals          []planfsm.Signal     // agent sentinel files found this tick
1948: 	WaveSignals      []planfsm.WaveSignal // implement-wave-N signal files found this tick
1949: 	TmuxSessionCount int                  // number of kas_-prefixed tmux sessions
1950: }
1951: 
1952: // tickUpdateMetadataCmd is the callback to update the metadata of the instances every 200ms. We iterate
1953: // over all instances and capture their output, but each tmux capture-pane call is <5ms so this is fine
1954: // even at 20 instances (~100ms total). 200ms gives 5 ticks/sec for responsive signal processing.
1955: var tickUpdateMetadataCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1956: 	time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)
1957: 	return tickUpdateMetadataMessage{}
1958: }
1959: 
1960: func (m *home) toastTickCmd() tea.Cmd {
1961: 	return func() tea.Msg {
1962: 		time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
1963: 		return overlay.ToastTickMsg{}
1964: 	}
1965: }
1966: 
1967: func (m *home) searchClickUp(query string) tea.Cmd {
1968: 	return func() tea.Msg {
1969: 		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(m.ctx, clickUpOpTimeout)
1970: 		defer cancel()
1971: 
1972: 		importer, err := m.getOrCreateImporter(ctx)
1973: 		if err != nil {
1974: 			return clickUpSearchResultMsg{Query: query, Err: normalizeClickUpError(err)}
1975: 		}
1976: 
1977: 		searchDone := make(chan clickUpSearchResultMsg, 1)
1978: 		go func() {
1979: 			results, searchErr := importer.Search(query)
1980: 			searchDone <- clickUpSearchResultMsg{Query: query, Results: results, Err: searchErr}
1981: 		}()
1982: 
1983: 		select {
1984: 		case msg := <-searchDone:
1985: 			msg.Err = normalizeClickUpError(msg.Err)
1986: 			if msg.Err != nil {
1987: 				// Don't nil the importer for MultipleWorkspacesError — we need
1988: 				// to call SetWorkspaceID on it after the user picks a workspace.
1989: 				var mwErr *clickup.MultipleWorkspacesError
1990: 				if errors.As(msg.Err, &mwErr) {
1991: 					// Resolve workspace IDs to names for a better picker UX.
1992: 					mwErr.WorkspaceNames = importer.FetchWorkspaceNames(mwErr.WorkspaceIDs)
1993: 				} else {
1994: 					m.clickUpImporter = nil // force re-init on next attempt
1995: 				}
1996: 			}
1997: 			return msg
1998: 		case <-ctx.Done():
1999: 			m.clickUpImporter = nil // force re-init on next attempt
2000: 			return clickUpSearchResultMsg{Query: query, Err: normalizeClickUpError(ctx.Err())}
2001: 		}
2002: 	}
2003: }
2004: 
2005: func (m *home) fetchClickUpTaskWithTimeout(taskID string) tea.Cmd {
2006: 	return func() tea.Msg {
2007: 		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(m.ctx, clickUpOpTimeout)
2008: 		defer cancel()
2009: 
2010: 		if m.clickUpImporter == nil {
2011: 			return clickUpTaskFetchedMsg{Err: fmt.Errorf("importer not initialized")}
2012: 		}
2013: 
2014: 		fetchDone := make(chan clickUpTaskFetchedMsg, 1)
2015: 		go func() {
2016: 			task, fetchErr := m.clickUpImporter.FetchTask(taskID)
2017: 			fetchDone <- clickUpTaskFetchedMsg{Task: task, Err: fetchErr}
2018: 		}()
2019: 
2020: 		select {
2021: 		case msg := <-fetchDone:
2022: 			msg.Err = normalizeClickUpError(msg.Err)
2023: 			if msg.Err != nil {
2024: 				m.clickUpImporter = nil // force re-init on next attempt
2025: 			}
2026: 			return msg
2027: 		case <-ctx.Done():
2028: 			m.clickUpImporter = nil // force re-init on next attempt
2029: 			return clickUpTaskFetchedMsg{Err: normalizeClickUpError(ctx.Err())}
2030: 		}
2031: 	}
2032: }
2033: 
2034: func normalizeClickUpError(err error) error {
2035: 	if err == nil {
2036: 		return nil
2037: 	}
2038: 	if errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
2039: 		return fmt.Errorf("operation canceled")
2040: 	}
2041: 	if errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) {
2042: 		return fmt.Errorf("operation timed out after %s", clickUpOpTimeout)
2043: 	}
2044: 	return err
2045: }
2046: 
2047: func (m *home) getOrCreateImporter(ctx context.Context) (*clickup.Importer, error) {
2048: 	if m.clickUpImporter != nil {
2049: 		return m.clickUpImporter, nil
2050: 	}
2051: 	if m.clickUpConfig == nil {
2052: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("no clickup MCP server configured")
2053: 	}
2054: 
2055: 	transport, err := m.createTransport(ctx, *m.clickUpConfig)
2056: 	if err != nil {
2057: 		return nil, err
2058: 	}
2059: 
2060: 	client, err := mcpclient.NewClient(transport)
2061: 	if err != nil {
2062: 		_ = transport.Close()
2063: 		return nil, err
2064: 	}
2065: 	if err := client.Initialize(); err != nil {
2066: 		_ = client.Close()
2067: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("MCP initialize: %w", err)
2068: 	}
2069: 	if _, err := client.ListTools(); err != nil {
2070: 		_ = client.Close()
2071: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("MCP list tools: %w", err)
2072: 	}
2073: 
2074: 	m.clickUpImporter = clickup.NewImporter(client)
2075: 
2076: 	// Restore saved workspace_id from per-project config.
2077: 	if projCfg := clickup.LoadProjectConfig(m.activeRepoPath); projCfg.WorkspaceID != "" {
2078: 		m.clickUpImporter.SetWorkspaceID(projCfg.WorkspaceID)
2079: 	}
2080: 
2081: 	return m.clickUpImporter, nil
2082: }
2083: 
2084: func (m *home) createTransport(ctx context.Context, cfg clickup.MCPServerConfig) (mcpclient.Transport, error) {
2085: 	switch cfg.Type {
2086: 	case "http":
2087: 		token, err := m.getClickUpToken(ctx)
2088: 		if err != nil {
2089: 			return nil, err
2090: 		}
2091: 		return mcpclient.NewHTTPTransport(cfg.URL, token), nil
2092: 	case "stdio":
2093: 		envSlice := make([]string, 0, len(cfg.Env))
2094: 		for k, v := range cfg.Env {
2095: 			envSlice = append(envSlice, k+"="+v)
2096: 		}
2097: 		return mcpclient.NewStdioTransport(cfg.Command, cfg.Args, envSlice)
2098: 	default:
2099: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported transport type: %s", cfg.Type)
2100: 	}
2101: }
2102: 
2103: func (m *home) getClickUpToken(ctx context.Context) (string, error) {
2104: 	// 1. Check opencode's mcp-auth.json first (populated by `opencode mcp auth clickup`).
2105: 	ocPath := mcpclient.OpencodeMCPAuthPath()
2106: 	if tok, err := mcpclient.LoadOpencodeToken(ocPath, "clickup"); err == nil && !tok.IsExpired() {
2107: 		return tok.AccessToken, nil
2108: 	}
2109: 
2110: 	// 2. Fall back to kasmos's own cached token.
2111: 	path := mcpclient.TokenPath()
2112: 	tok, err := mcpclient.LoadToken(path)
2113: 	if err == nil && !tok.IsExpired() {
2114: 		return tok.AccessToken, nil
2115: 	}
2116: 
2117: 	// 3. Last resort: run our own OAuth flow.
2118: 	oauthCfg := mcpclient.OAuthConfig{
2119: 		AuthURL:  "https://app.clickup.com/api",
2120: 		TokenURL: "https://api.clickup.com/api/v2/oauth/token",
2121: 		ClientID: "kasmos", // TODO: register ClickUp OAuth app
2122: 	}
2123: 	tok, err = mcpclient.OAuthFlow(ctx, oauthCfg, nil)
2124: 	if err != nil {
2125: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("oauth: %w", err)
2126: 	}
2127: 	if err := mcpclient.SaveToken(path, tok); err != nil {
2128: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("save token: %w", err)
2129: 	}
2130: 	return tok.AccessToken, nil
2131: }
2132: 
2133: func detectClickUpCmd(repoPath string) tea.Cmd {
2134: 	return func() tea.Msg {
2135: 		claudeDir := filepath.Join(os.Getenv("HOME"), ".claude")
2136: 		cfg, found := clickup.DetectMCP(repoPath, claudeDir)
2137: 		if !found {
2138: 			return nil
2139: 		}
2140: 		return clickUpDetectedMsg{Config: cfg}
2141: 	}
2142: }

45: // ContextMenuItem represents a single menu option.
46: type ContextMenuItem struct {
47: 	Label    string
48: 	Action   string // identifier returned when selected
49: 	Disabled bool
50: }
51: 
52: // ContextMenu displays a floating context menu with search and numbered shortcuts.
53: type ContextMenu struct {
54: 	items       []ContextMenuItem
55: 	filtered    []filteredItem
56: 	selectedIdx int
57: 	x, y        int // screen position
58: 	width       int
59: 	searchQuery string
60: }
61: 
62: // filteredItem tracks the original index for number shortcuts.
63: type filteredItem struct {
64: 	item    ContextMenuItem
65: 	origIdx int // 1-based number for display and hotkey
66: }
67: 
68: // NewContextMenu creates a context menu at the given screen position.
69: func NewContextMenu(x, y int, items []ContextMenuItem) *ContextMenu {
70: 	c := &ContextMenu{
71: 		items: items,
72: 		x:     x,
73: 		y:     y,
74: 	}
75: 	c.applyFilter()
76: 	c.calculateWidth()
77: 	return c
78: }
79: 
80: func (c *ContextMenu) calculateWidth() {
81: 	maxWidth := 0
82: 	for i, item := range c.items {
83: 		label := fmt.Sprintf("%d %s", i+1, item.Label)
84: 		if len(label) > maxWidth {
85: 			maxWidth = len(label)
86: 		}
87: 	}
88: 	placeholder := "\uf002 Type to filter..."
89: 	if len(placeholder) > maxWidth {
90: 		maxWidth = len(placeholder)
91: 	}
92: 	c.width = maxWidth + 4 // padding
93: }
94: 
95: func (c *ContextMenu) applyFilter() {
96: 	c.filtered = nil
97: 	query := strings.ToLower(c.searchQuery)
98: 	for i, item := range c.items {
99: 		if query == "" || strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(item.Label), query) {
100: 			c.filtered = append(c.filtered, filteredItem{
101: 				item:    item,
102: 				origIdx: i + 1,
103: 			})
104: 		}
105: 	}
106: 	if c.selectedIdx >= len(c.filtered) {
107: 		c.selectedIdx = len(c.filtered) - 1
108: 	}
109: 	if c.selectedIdx < 0 {
110: 		c.selectedIdx = 0
111: 	}
112: 	c.skipToNonDisabled(1)
113: }
114: 
115: func (c *ContextMenu) skipToNonDisabled(direction int) {
116: 	if len(c.filtered) == 0 {
117: 		return
118: 	}
119: 	start := c.selectedIdx
120: 	for c.filtered[c.selectedIdx].item.Disabled {
121: 		c.selectedIdx += direction
122: 		if c.selectedIdx >= len(c.filtered) {
123: 			c.selectedIdx = 0
124: 		}
125: 		if c.selectedIdx < 0 {
126: 			c.selectedIdx = len(c.filtered) - 1
127: 		}
128: 		if c.selectedIdx == start {
129: 			break
130: 		}
131: 	}
132: }
133: 
134: // HandleKeyPress processes key events. Returns the selected action string, or "" if no selection.
135: // Returns ("", false) if menu stays open, (action, true) if an item was selected, ("", true) if dismissed.
136: func (c *ContextMenu) HandleKeyPress(msg tea.KeyMsg) (string, bool) {
137: 	switch msg.String() {
138: 	case "esc":
139: 		return "", true
140: 	case " ", "enter":
141: 		if c.selectedIdx < len(c.filtered) && !c.filtered[c.selectedIdx].item.Disabled {
142: 			return c.filtered[c.selectedIdx].item.Action, true
143: 		}
144: 		return "", false
145: 	case "up":
146: 		if len(c.filtered) > 0 {
147: 			start := c.selectedIdx
148: 			for {
149: 				c.selectedIdx--
150: 				if c.selectedIdx < 0 {
151: 					c.selectedIdx = len(c.filtered) - 1
152: 				}
153: 				if !c.filtered[c.selectedIdx].item.Disabled || c.selectedIdx == start {
154: 					break
155: 				}
156: 			}
157: 		}
158: 	case "down":
159: 		if len(c.filtered) > 0 {
160: 			start := c.selectedIdx
161: 			for {
162: 				c.selectedIdx++
163: 				if c.selectedIdx >= len(c.filtered) {
164: 					c.selectedIdx = 0
165: 				}
166: 				if !c.filtered[c.selectedIdx].item.Disabled || c.selectedIdx == start {
167: 					break
168: 				}
169: 			}
170: 		}
171: 	case "backspace":
172: 		if len(c.searchQuery) > 0 {
173: 			runes := []rune(c.searchQuery)
174: 			c.searchQuery = string(runes[:len(runes)-1])
175: 			c.applyFilter()
176: 		}
177: 	default:
178: 		if msg.Type == tea.KeyRunes {
179: 			r := msg.Runes[0]
180: 			// Number shortcut (1-9) when search is empty
181: 			if r >= '1' && r <= '9' && c.searchQuery == "" {
182: 				num := int(r - '0')
183: 				for i, fi := range c.filtered {
184: 					if fi.origIdx == num && !fi.item.Disabled {
185: 						c.selectedIdx = i
186: 						return fi.item.Action, true
187: 					}
188: 				}
189: 				return "", false
190: 			}
191: 			c.searchQuery += string(msg.Runes)
192: 			c.applyFilter()
193: 		}
194: 	}
195: 	return "", false
196: }
197: 
198: // Render returns the styled menu string.
199: func (c *ContextMenu) Render() string {
200: 	var b strings.Builder
201: 
202: 	// Search bar
203: 	innerWidth := c.width
204: 	if innerWidth < 10 {
205: 		innerWidth = 10
206: 	}
207: 	searchText := c.searchQuery
208: 	if searchText == "" {
209: 		searchText = contextSearchPlaceholderStyle.Render("\uf002 Type to filter...")
210: 	}
211: 	b.WriteString(contextSearchStyle.Width(innerWidth).Render(searchText))
212: 	b.WriteString("\n")
213: 
214: 	// Items with numbers
215: 	if len(c.filtered) == 0 {
216: 		b.WriteString(contextDisabledStyle.Width(c.width).Render("No matches"))
217: 	} else {
218: 		for i, fi := range c.filtered {
219: 			numPrefix := contextNumberStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("%d", fi.origIdx))

221: 
222: 			var line string

226: 			} else if i == c.selectedIdx {
227: 				line = contextSelectedStyle.Width(c.width).Render(
228: 					fmt.Sprintf("%d %s", fi.origIdx, fi.item.Label))
229: 			} else {

parts.text:
## Goal

Implement a **restart-agent** feature: add a "restart" option to the instance context menu (the space menu where "open", "kill", etc. appear) that kills the existing tmux session (if it still exists) and launches a fresh agent with the same configuration and prompt.

## Instructions

- Use `kasmos-coder` and `cli-tools` skills (loaded at start of conversation)
- Follow TDD: write failing tests FIRST, then implement
- Use `rg` not `grep`, `sd` not `sed`, etc.
- Do NOT use `git add .` — only add specific files
- The skill is loaded from `/home/kas/dev/kasmos/.opencode/skills/`

## Discoveries

### Codebase Architecture
- **Instance context menu** is built in `openContextMenu()` in `app/app_actions.go` (~line 573)
- **Context actions** are handled in `executeContextAction()` in `app/app_actions.go`
- The context menu shows: "open", "kill", "pause"/"resume", "focus agent", "rename", "push branch", "create pr", and optionally "mark complete" for wave tasks
- The message type for async instance startup is `instanceStartedMsg` (defined in `app/app.go` ~line 1912)
- `instanceChangedMsg` (line ~1816 of `app/app.go`) is used to refresh the UI after instance mutations
- `confirmAction()` in `app/app_input.go` sets `m.state = stateConfirm` and `m.pendingConfirmAction`

### Session/Instance Structure
- `Instance` is in `session/instance.go` — key fields: `Title`, `Path`, `Program`, `PlanFile`, `AgentType`, `Branch`, `SkipPermissions`, `AutoYes`, `QueuedPrompt`, `started` (private), `tmuxSession`, `gitWorktree`
- `Kill()` terminates BOTH tmux session AND git worktree
- `StopTmux()` terminates tmux only, no worktree touch
- `Start()`, `StartOnMainBranch()`, `StartInSharedWorktree()`, `StartOnBranch()` are lifecycle methods in `session/instance_lifecycle.go`
- `transferPromptToCli()` moves `QueuedPrompt` → `tmuxSession.SetInitialPrompt()` for supported programs (opencode)
- For restart: we need to stop tmux only (preserve worktree), reset state flags, create new tmux session in same directory

### Prompt Reconstruction for Plan Agents
- **Planner**: `buildPlanPrompt(planName, entry.Description)` 
- **Coder**: `buildImplementPrompt(planFile)`
- **Reviewer**: `scaffold.LoadReviewPrompt("docs/plans/"+planFile, planName)`
- **Solo/ad-hoc**: no specific prompt needed

### Test Infrastructure
- `newTestHome()` creates a minimal home struct for testing
- `tmux.NewTmuxSessionWithDeps(name, program, skipPermissions, ptyFactory, cmdExec)` for mock tmux
- `testPtyFactory` is defined in `session/instance_session_async_test.go` (available to all session package tests)
- Tests live in `package app` (same package) and `package session` (same package)

### LSP Error Found in Tests
- `h.nav.AddInstance(inst)()` — `AddInstance()` returns a `func()` (a finalizer/cleanup func), calling it returns no value. The test code uses it as `_ = h.nav.AddInstance(inst)()` which tries to assign the result of calling the returned func. Fix: should be `h.nav.AddInstance(inst)()` (just call it without assignment) or `h.nav.AddInstance(inst)` (don't call the returned func at all).

## Accomplished

### Completed
1. **Explored codebase** — read all relevant files to understand structure

### In Progress (Tests Written but Implementation Missing)
2. **Failing tests written** in `session/instance_lifecycle_test.go`:
   - `TestRestartTmux_FailsWhenNotStarted` — verify error when not started
   - `TestRestartTmux_ResetsStateAndRestartsSession` — verify state flags reset + session restarted  
   - `TestRestartTmux_TransfersQueuedPrompt` — verify prompt transferred for opencode

3. **Failing tests written** in `app/app_restart_test.go` (new file):
   - `TestOpenContextMenu_InstanceIncludesRestart` — verify "restart" appears in context menu
   - `TestExecuteContextAction_RestartInstance_ShowsConfirmation` — verify restart shows confirm dialog
   - `TestExecuteContextAction_RestartInstance_NilSelected` — verify nil-safe no-op

   **⚠️ LSP Error in app_restart_test.go**: Lines 23 and 53 use `_ = h.nav.AddInstance(inst)()` which causes "no value used as value" error. Fix: change to just `h.nav.AddInstance(inst)()` without the `_ =` assignment.

### Still To Do
4. **Implement `RestartTmux()` method** in `session/instance_lifecycle.go` — the core restart logic:
   ```go
   func (i *Instance) RestartTmux() error {
       if !i.started { return error }
       _ = i.tmuxSession.Close()  // best-effort
       ts := tmux.NewTmuxSession(i.Title, i.Program, i.SkipPermissions)
       ts.SetAgentType(i.AgentType)
       i.tmuxSession = ts
       i.setTmuxTaskEnv()
       i.configureSessionTitle()
       i.transferPromptToCli()
       workDir := i.Path
       if i.gitWorktree != nil { workDir = i.gitWorktree.GetWorktreePath() }
       if err := ts.Start(workDir); err != nil { return err }
       i.Exited = false; i.ImplementationComplete = false; i.HasWorked = false
       i.AwaitingWork = false; i.PromptDetected = false
       i.SetStatus(Running)
       return nil
   }
   ```

5. **Add "restart" to instance context menu** in `app/app_actions.go` (`openContextMenu()` ~line 591):
   ```go
   {Label: "restart", Action: "restart_instance"},
   ```
   Add after `{Label: "kill", Action: "kill_instance"}`.

6. **Add `restart_instance` case** in `executeContextAction()` in `app/app_actions.go`:
   - Get selected instance
   - Reconstruct prompt based on `selected.AgentType` and `selected.PlanFile`
   - Show confirm dialog via `m.confirmAction(fmt.Sprintf("restart '%s'?", title), restartCmd)`
   - `restartCmd` sets `QueuedPrompt` then calls `selected.RestartTmux()`

7. **Run tests** to verify all pass:
   ```bash
   go test ./session/... -run "TestRestartTmux"
   go test ./app/... -run "TestRestart\|TestOpenContextMenu"
   ```

8. **Commit** the changes

## Relevant files / directories

| File                                   | Status                          | Purpose                                                        |
| -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `app/app_actions.go`                     | To edit                         | Add "restart" to context menu + handle `restart_instance` action |
| `app/app_restart_test.go`                | Created (has LSP errors to fix) | Tests for context menu and action                              |
| `session/instance_lifecycle.go`          | To edit                         | Add `RestartTmux()` method                                       |
| `session/instance_lifecycle_test.go`     | Edited (tests added, failing)   | Tests for `RestartTmux`                                          |
| `session/instance.go`                    | Read                            | Instance struct definition                                     |
| `session/instance_session_async_test.go` | Read                            | Contains `testPtyFactory` for tests                              |
| `app/app.go`                             | Read                            | Message types, Update handler                                  |
| `app/app_state.go`                       | Read                            | `spawnPlanAgent`, `spawnReviewer`, `buildImplementPrompt`, etc.      |
| `session/tmux/tmux.go`                   | Read                            | `NewTmuxSessionWithDeps` for testing                             |
| `ui/overlay/contextMenu.go`              | Read                            | `ContextMenu.Items()` method                                     |
Continue if you have next steps, or stop and ask for clarification if you are unsure how to proceed.

<skill_content name="kasmos-coder">
# Skill: kasmos-coder

# kasmos-coder

You implement code. One task (managed) or a full plan sequentially (manual). Your disciplines are TDD, systematic debugging, evidence-first verification, and safe shared-worktree hygiene. Review feedback gets technical evaluation, not performative agreement.

---

## CLI Tools Hard Gate

<HARD-GATE>
### Banned Tools

These legacy tools are NEVER permitted. Using them is a violation, not a preference.

| Banned | Replacement | No Exceptions |
|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `grep` | `rg` (ripgrep) | Even for simple one-liners |
| `grep -r` | `rg` | Recursive grep is still grep |
| `grep -E` | `rg` | Extended regex is still grep |
| `sed` | `sd` | Even for one-liners |
| `awk` | `yq`/`jq` (structured) or `sd` (text) | No awk for any purpose |
| `find` | `fd` or glob tools | Even for simple file listing |
| `diff` (standalone) | `difft` | `git diff` is fine — standalone `diff` is not |
| `wc -l` | `scc` | Even for single files |

**`git diff` is allowed** — it's a git subcommand, not standalone `diff`.

**STOP.** If you are about to type `grep`, `sed`, `awk`, `find`, `diff`, or `wc` — stop. There are no exceptions.
</HARD-GATE>

### Tool Selection

| Task | Use | Not |
|------|-----|-----|
| Find literal string | `rg` | `grep` |
| Find files by name/ext | `fd` | `find` |
| Replace string in files | `sd` | `sed` |
| Rename symbol across files | `ast-grep` | `sed`/`sd` |
| Structural code rewrite | `comby` | `sed`/`awk` |
| Find code pattern | `ast-grep --pattern` | `rg` |
| Read/modify YAML/TOML/JSON | `yq` / `jq` | `sed`/`awk` |
| Review code changes | `difft` | `diff` |
| Spell check code | `typos` | manual |
| Count lines / codebase metrics | `scc` | `wc -l` |

---

## Where You Fit

### Env Vars

| Variable | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| `KASMOS_MANAGED` | Set to `1` when kasmos TUI spawned you. Unset in raw terminal. |
| `KASMOS_TASK` | The task number you're assigned (managed only). |
| `KASMOS_WAVE` | The wave number (managed only). |
| `KASMOS_PEERS` | Number of sibling agents running in parallel. `0` or unset = solo. |

### Managed (`KASMOS_MANAGED=1`)

kasmos spawned you to implement **one specific task** identified by `KASMOS_TASK`. Your scope:

1. Read the plan content from the plan store (via `kas plan` CLI or the plan content API). Find your wave (`KASMOS_WAVE`) and task (`KASMOS_TASK`).
2. Implement that single task following TDD discipline below.
3. Commit your work with task number in the commit message.
4. **Stop.** Do not implement other tasks — they belong to sibling agents or future waves.

**Do NOT write the implement-finished sentinel.** kasmos detects task completion via prompt
detection (when your agent returns to its input prompt) and handles the
implementing→reviewing transition through the wave orchestrator. Writing implement-finished
from a task agent prematurely triggers review and breaks wave orchestration.

### Manual (KASMOS_MANAGED unset)

You execute the full plan sequentially, wave by wave:

1. Read the plan. Check its **Size** field to determine wave structure.
2. Create a todo list with one item per task (all `pending`), then begin.
3. Execute one wave at a time: implement each task (TDD), commit, then proceed to next task.
4. After each wave completes, self-review before starting the next wave.
5. When all waves complete, write the implement-finished sentinel (see **Signaling**).

---

## TDD Discipline

### The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Wrote code before writing the test? Delete it. Start over from the test. No exceptions:
- Don't keep it "for reference" — you'll adapt it, which is testing after
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests — delete means delete
- Implement fresh from tests

### RED → GREEN → REFACTOR

**RED — Write the failing test**

Write one minimal test showing the required behavior. Run it. Confirm:
- Test actually fails (not errors from syntax — a genuine test failure)
- Failure message is the expected one ("function not found", "assertion error")
- Test fails because the feature is missing, not a typo

**Never skip the RED verification.** If the test passes immediately, you are testing existing behavior. Fix the test.

**GREEN — Minimal implementation**

Write the simplest code that makes the test pass. Don't add features. Don't refactor other code. Don't "improve" beyond what the test requires. Verify:
- Your test passes
- All other tests still pass
- No new errors or warnings

**REFACTOR — Clean up**

Only after green: remove duplication, improve names, extract helpers. Never add behavior during refactor. Keep tests green throughout.

**Repeat** — one test per behavior. When the task is fully implemented, all new tests are green and no existing tests are broken.

### Red Flags — STOP and Start Over

| Thought | Action |
|---------|--------|
| "I'll write tests after to verify it works" | Delete code, write test first |
| "Too simple to need a test" | Write the test — it takes 30 seconds |
| "I already manually tested it" | Manual ≠ systematic. Write the test |
| "I'll keep this code as reference" | Delete it. Keeping = adapting = testing after |
| "TDD will slow me down" | Debugging later is slower. Write the test |
| "Just this once" | Not a valid exception |

---

## Shared Worktree Safety

When `KASMOS_PEERS > 0`, sibling agents are modifying this worktree concurrently.

**Never:**
- `git add .` or `git add -A` — you will stage a sibling's in-progress files
- `git stash` — you will destroy sibling's uncommitted changes
- `git reset` — you will destroy sibling's uncommitted changes
- `git checkout -- <file>` on files you didn't modify — you will revert a sibling's edits
- Run project-wide formatters (`go fmt ./...`, `prettier --write .`) — scope to your files only
- Run project-wide linters across files you didn't touch

**Always:**
- `git add <specific-files>` — only the files you changed
- Include task number in every commit message: `feat(task-6): add kasmos-coder skill`
- Expect untracked files and dirty state from siblings — ignore them
- Run tests scoped to your changed packages where possible before committing

**When you see test failures in files outside your task scope:** Do not attempt to fix them. They may be caused by incomplete parallel work from a sibling agent. Report the failure context in your signal and stop.

---

## Debugging Discipline

When tests fail, builds break, or behavior is unexpected — investigate before proposing fixes. Random patches mask the actual problem and create new ones.

### The Four Phases

**Phase 1 — Root Cause Investigation**

Before attempting any fix:
1. Read error messages completely — stack traces, line numbers, error codes
2. Reproduce the failure consistently — what exact steps trigger it?
3. Check recent changes — git diff, recent commits, env differences
4. In multi-component systems: add diagnostic instrumentation at each boundary to find exactly where it breaks. Run once to gather evidence, then analyze.
5. Trace data flow — where does the bad value originate? Trace backward up the call stack to the source.

**Phase 2 — Pattern Analysis**

Find working examples in the codebase doing something similar. Compare them against the broken code. List every difference — don't assume "that can't matter." Understand all dependencies (config, env, state).

**Phase 3 — Hypothesis and Testing**

Form one specific hypothesis: "I think X is the root cause because Y." Test it with the smallest possible change. One variable at a time. Verify — did it work? If not, form a new hypothesis. Don't stack fixes.

**Phase 4 — Implementation**

Write a failing test reproducing the bug first (TDD discipline applies to bugfixes). Implement the single fix addressing the root cause. Verify the test now passes and no other tests regressed.

**If the fix doesn't work:** Return to Phase 1. Re-analyze with new information.

**If 3 fixes have failed:** STOP. Question the architecture — each fix revealing a new problem in a different place is an architectural signal, not a debugging problem. Escalate or document the situation rather than attempting fix #4.

### Red Flags — Return to Phase 1

- "Quick fix for now, investigate later"
- "Just try changing X and see if it works"
- "It's probably X, let me fix that"
- "I don't fully understand but this might work"
- Proposing solutions before tracing data flow
- "One more fix attempt" (when you've already tried 2+)
- Each fix reveals a new problem in a different place

---

## Verification

Before claiming a task is complete, before committing, before writing the finished sentinel:

### The Gate

```
1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this works?
2. RUN: Execute it fresh. Complete output.
3. READ: Full output. Check exit code. Count failures.
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence.
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence.
5. ONLY THEN: Claim completion.
```

Skipping any step is not verification — it's guessing.

### Common Verification Commands

```bash
# Go
go build ./...
go test ./...
go test ./path/to/package/...   # scoped — preferred in shared worktree

# Node
npm test
npm run build

# Python
pytest
pytest path/to/tests/

# General
go vet ./...
typos                            # spell check before committing
```

### Red Flags — STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to" before verification
- Claiming tests pass without running them in this message
- Trusting previous run output — always run fresh
- Expressing satisfaction ("Done!", "Perfect!") before the verification command output is in view
- Committing without a clean build

---

## Handling Reviewer Feedback

Code review requires technical evaluation, not performative agreement.

### Response Pattern

```
1. READ: Complete feedback without reacting
2. UNDERSTAND: Restate the technical requirement (or ask if unclear)
3. VERIFY: Check against the actual codebase
4. EVALUATE: Is this technically sound for THIS codebase?
5. RESPOND: Technical acknowledgment, or reasoned pushback with evidence
6. IMPLEMENT: One item at a time, test each
```

### Forbidden Responses

**Never:**
- "You're absolutely right!" — performative
- "Great point!" / "Excellent feedback!" — performative
- "Let me implement that now" — before verification

**Instead:**
- Restate the technical requirement
- Ask for clarification if anything is unclear
- Push back with technical reasoning if the suggestion is wrong
- Just start working — actions over words

### Before Implementing Any Item

- Unclear about any item? **Stop. Ask for clarification on all unclear items first.** Do not implement partial feedback — items may be related.
- Does it break existing functionality? Verify before proceeding.
- Does it violate YAGNI? Check if the feature is actually used (`rg` in codebase).
- Does it conflict with architectural decisions already made? Escalate rather than blindly implement.

### When to Push Back

Push back when:
- The suggestion breaks existing functionality (show the test)
- The reviewer lacks full context for the decision
- It violates YAGNI (feature not used anywhere)
- It's technically incorrect for this stack/version
- It conflicts with established architectural decisions

**How:** Technical reasoning, not defensiveness. Reference working tests. Reference the code.

### Implementation Order

For multi-item feedback:
1. Clarify all unclear items first
2. Blocking issues (breaks, security holes)
3. Simple fixes (typos, wrong identifiers, missing imports)
4. Complex fixes (refactoring, logic changes)
5. Test each fix individually before moving to the next
6. Verify no regressions after all items complete

### Acknowledging Correct Feedback

```
✅ "Fixed. [One sentence describing the change]"
✅ "Good catch — [specific issue]. Fixed in [location]."
✅ [Just fix it. The code shows you heard the feedback.]

❌ "You're absolutely right!"
❌ "Thanks for catching that!"
❌ Any gratitude expression
```

---

## Signaling

### Managed (`KASMOS_MANAGED=1`)

**Do NOT write any sentinel files.** kasmos detects task completion automatically when your
agent returns to its input prompt. The wave orchestrator handles all lifecycle transitions
(implementing→reviewing) and reviewer spawning.

After implementing and committing your task: **stop.** Do not implement other tasks, do not
write signal files, do not invoke branch finishing — kasmos handles orchestration.

**Do not modify plan state directly.**

### Manual (KASMOS_MANAGED unset)

Execute waves sequentially:

1. Implement all tasks in Wave 1 (TDD, commit each task)
2. Self-review Wave 1 before proceeding — run full tests, check for regressions
3. Implement Wave 2, self-review, continue through all waves
4. After all waves complete and all tests pass:

```bash
# Transition the plan to "reviewing" via CLI
kas plan transition <plan-file> request_review
```

Then handle branch finishing — present these options to the user:

```
implementation complete. what would you like to do?

1. merge back to <base-branch> locally
2. push and create a pull request
3. keep the branch as-is (handle it later)
4. discard this work

which option?
```

**Option 1 — Merge locally (worktree-safe):**
```bash
# First, rebase onto latest main so the merge is a fast-forward
git fetch origin <base-branch>
git rebase origin/<base-branch>
<run tests on rebased result>

# Merge from the ROOT worktree (that's where <base-branch> is checked out)
ROOT_WORKTREE="$(git worktree list --porcelain | head -1 | sd '^worktree ' '')"
git -C "$ROOT_WORKTREE" merge <feature-branch>

# Clean up
git worktree remove <worktree-path>
git branch -d <feature-branch>
```
> **Why not `git checkout <base-branch>` here?** In a worktree layout the base branch is
> already checked out in the root worktree. Git prevents a branch from being checked out
> in two worktrees simultaneously. Instead, run the merge *from* the root worktree using
> `git -C`.

**Option 2 — Push and create PR:**
```bash
git push -u origin <feature-branch>
gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## summary
- <what changed>
- <why>

## test plan
- [ ] <verification steps>
EOF
)"
```
Keep the worktree until the PR merges.

**Option 3 — Keep as-is:** Report path. Don't clean up. Don't update plan state.

**Option 4 — Discard:** Confirm first with exact text `discard`. Then:
```bash
# Remove worktree first (must leave it before deleting), then delete the branch
git worktree remove <worktree-path>
git branch -D <feature-branch>
```

Update plan status to `done` after options 1, 2, or 4:
```bash
kas plan set-status <plan-file> done --force
```

---

## Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| Writing production code before a failing test | Delete the code, write the test first |
| Claiming tests pass without running them | Run the command, read the output |
| `git add .` in a shared worktree | `git add <specific-files>` only |
| Attempting fix #4 after 3 have failed | Stop — it's architecture, not a bug |
| Performative agreement with reviewer | Technical verification, then act |
| Implementing unclear review feedback | Ask for clarification on ALL unclear items first |
| Running project-wide formatters | Scope formatters to your changed files only |
| Modifying plan state directly when `KASMOS_MANAGED=1` | Write sentinel file instead — kasmos handles state |
| Implementing sibling tasks in managed mode | Implement ONE task (KASMOS_TASK), then signal and stop |

Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
